file.content:
# Multi-platform release workflow (macOS, Windows, Linux)
#
# Builds and publishes the Electron app for all platforms via GitHub Releases.
# electron-updater reads latest-mac.yml / latest.yml / latest-linux.yml from
# the release assets to deliver auto-updates.
#
# Trigger: GitHub Actions UI → "Run workflow" → pick bump type
# Or CLI:  gh workflow run release.yml -f bump=patch
#          gh workflow run release.yml -f bump=minor
#          gh workflow run release.yml -f bump=patch -f dry_run=true
#
# Required GitHub Secrets:
#   APPLE_CERTIFICATE                  — Base64-encoded .p12 Developer ID Application certificate
#   APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD         — Password for the .p12 file
#   APPLE_ID                           — Apple ID email for notarization
#   APPLE_PASSWORD                     — App-specific password (NOT your Apple ID password)
#   APPLE_TEAM_ID                      — 10-character Apple Developer Team ID
#
# Optional GitHub Secrets:
#   SENTRY_DSN_NODE                    — Sentry DSN for backend error tracking
#   VITE_SENTRY_DSN                    — Sentry DSN for frontend error tracking
#   SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN                  — Sentry auth token for source map upload

name: Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      bump:
        description: "Version bump type"
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
      dry_run:
        description: "Dry run (build only, skip GitHub Release)"
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  # ── Step 1: Validate version & bump files ───────────────────────────
  validate-and-bump:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.version }}
      tag: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.tag }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Ensure release runs from main
        if: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false }}
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref_name }}" != "main" ]]; then
            echo "::error::Release must be triggered from main branch, got: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Compute next version
        id: validate
        env:
          BUMP: ${{ inputs.bump }}
        run: |
          CURRENT=$(jq -r '.version' package.json)
          IFS='.' read -r MAJOR MINOR PATCH <<< "$CURRENT"
          case "$BUMP" in
            patch) PATCH=$((PATCH + 1)) ;;
            minor) MINOR=$((MINOR + 1)); PATCH=0 ;;
            major) MAJOR=$((MAJOR + 1)); MINOR=0; PATCH=0 ;;
          esac
          VERSION="${MAJOR}.${MINOR}.${PATCH}"
          TAG="v${VERSION}"

          echo "Current: $CURRENT → Next: $VERSION ($BUMP bump)"

          if [[ "${{ inputs.dry_run }}" != "true" ]] && git ls-remote --exit-code --tags origin "refs/tags/$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "::error::Tag $TAG already exists on remote"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.2.19

      - name: Bump version, commit & create tag
        if: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false }}
        run: |
          bash scripts/bump-version.sh "${{ steps.validate.outputs.version }}"
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add package.json
          git commit -m "release: v${{ steps.validate.outputs.version }}"
          git tag "${{ steps.validate.outputs.tag }}"
          git push --atomic origin HEAD "${{ steps.validate.outputs.tag }}"

  # ── Step 2a: Build macOS (arm64 + x64), sign, notarize ─────────────
  build-macos:
    needs: validate-and-bump
    runs-on: macos-14
    env:
      SENTRY_DSN_NODE: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN_NODE }}
      VITE_SENTRY_DSN: ${{ secrets.VITE_SENTRY_DSN }}
      SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false && needs.validate-and-bump.outputs.tag || github.ref }}

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.2.19

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build all
        run: bun run build:all

      - name: Package macOS (arm64 + x64)
        run: bunx electron-builder --mac --publish never
        env:
          CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERTIFICATE }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: macos
          path: |
            dist-electron/*.dmg
            dist-electron/*.zip
            dist-electron/*.blockmap
            dist-electron/latest-mac.yml
          if-no-files-found: error

  # ── Step 2b: Build Windows (x64) ───────────────────────────────────
  build-windows:
    needs: validate-and-bump
    runs-on: windows-latest
    env:
      SENTRY_DSN_NODE: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN_NODE }}
      VITE_SENTRY_DSN: ${{ secrets.VITE_SENTRY_DSN }}
      SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false && needs.validate-and-bump.outputs.tag || github.ref }}

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.2.19

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build all
        run: bun run build:all

      - name: Package Windows (x64)
        run: bunx electron-builder --win --publish never

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: windows
          path: |
            dist-electron/*.exe
            dist-electron/*.blockmap
            dist-electron/latest.yml
          if-no-files-found: error

  # ── Step 2c: Build Linux (x64) ─────────────────────────────────────
  build-linux:
    needs: validate-and-bump
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SENTRY_DSN_NODE: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN_NODE }}
      VITE_SENTRY_DSN: ${{ secrets.VITE_SENTRY_DSN }}
      SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false && needs.validate-and-bump.outputs.tag || github.ref }}

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.2.19

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build all
        run: bun run build:all

      - name: Package Linux (x64)
        run: bunx electron-builder --linux --publish never

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: linux
          path: |
            dist-electron/*.AppImage
            dist-electron/*.deb
            dist-electron/*.blockmap
            dist-electron/latest-linux.yml
          if-no-files-found: error

  # ── Step 3: Create GitHub Release with all artifacts ────────────────
  create-release:
    needs: [validate-and-bump, build-macos, build-windows, build-linux]
    if: ${{ inputs.dry_run == false }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts

      - name: Collect release files
        run: |
          mkdir -p release
          find artifacts -type f \( \
            -name "*.dmg" -o -name "*.zip" -o -name "*.exe" -o \
            -name "*.AppImage" -o -name "*.deb" -o \
            -name "*.blockmap" -o -name "*.yml" \
          \) -exec cp {} release/ \;
          echo "=== Release files ==="
          ls -lh release/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.validate-and-bump.outputs.tag }}
          VERSION: ${{ needs.validate-and-bump.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          gh release create "$TAG" \
            --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
            --title "Deus v${VERSION}" \
            --generate-notes \
            release/*

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Preload Script — Main Window
     3→ *
     4→ * Exposes a typed API to the renderer via contextBridge.
     5→ * This is the ONLY way the renderer communicates with the main process.
     6→ *
     7→ * Security: contextIsolation=true, nodeIntegration=false, sandbox=false (ESM preload requires sandbox=false).
     8→ * The renderer has zero direct access to Node.js or Electron APIs.
     9→ *
    10→ * The generic invoke/on/send bridge is guarded by an allowlist so the renderer
    11→ * cannot call arbitrary ipcMain handlers — only the channels listed below.
    12→ */
    13→
    14→import { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } from "electron";
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// IPC channel allowlists — ONLY these channels may be used via the generic
    18→// invoke/on/send bridge. Every channel MUST have a registered ipcMain.handle()
    19→// or ipcMain.on() in the main process. Update these sets when adding new IPC.
    20→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→
    22→const ALLOWED_INVOKE_CHANNELS = new Set([
    23→  // Window visibility / onboarding
    24→  "show_main_window",
    25→  "enter_onboarding_mode",
    26→  "exit_onboarding_mode",
    27→
    28→  // Folder dialog (snake_case alias)
    29→  "show_folder_dialog",
    30→
    31→  // Backend connection
    32→  "get_backend_port",
    33→
    34→  // CLI / environment checks (snake_case aliases)
    35→  "check_cli_tool",
    36→  "check_gh_auth",
    37→  "get_installed_apps",
    38→  "open_in_app",
    39→
    40→  // Browser webview management
    41→  "create_browser_webview",
    42→  "close_browser_webview",
    43→  "show_browser_webview",
    44→  "hide_browser_webview",
    45→  "hide_all_browser_webviews",
    46→  "set_browser_webview_bounds",
    47→  "navigate_browser_webview",
    48→  "reload_browser_webview",
    49→  "eval_browser_webview",
    50→  "eval_browser_webview_with_result",
    51→  "screenshot_browser_webview",
    52→  "open_browser_devtools",
    53→  "close_browser_devtools",
    54→  "browser:back",
    55→  "browser:forward",
    56→
    57→  // Browser detached windows
    58→  "browser:createDetachedWindow",
    59→  "browser:closeDetachedWindow",
    60→
    61→  // Native operations (called via generic invoke from platform layer)
    62→  "native:pickFolder",
    63→  "native:setZoom",
    64→  "native:setTitle",
    65→  "native:homeDir",
    66→]);
    67→
    68→const ALLOWED_EVENT_CHANNELS = new Set([
    69→  // Window state
    70→  "fullscreen-change",
    71→
    72→  // Backend lifecycle
    73→  "backend:port-changed",
    74→
    75→  // Workspace progress
    76→  "workspace:progress",
    77→
    78→  // File system events
    79→  "fs:changed",
    80→
    81→  // PTY events
    82→  "pty-data",
    83→  "pty-exit",
    84→
    85→  // Browser automation events
    86→  "browser:page-load",
    87→  "browser:title-changed",
    88→  "browser:url-change",
    89→  "browser-window:workspace-change",
    90→  "browser:detached-closed",
    91→  "browser:keyboard-shortcut",
    92→
    93→  // Simulator events
    94→  "sim:build-log",
    95→
    96→  // Chat insert events
    97→  "chat-insert",
    98→
    99→  // Git operations
   100→  "git-clone-progress",
   101→
   102→  // Auto-update state
   103→  "update:state",
   104→]);
   105→
   106→const electronAPI = {
   107→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→  // Backend connection info (renderer needs this to connect WebSocket)
   109→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→  getBackendPort: (): Promise<number> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:getBackendPort"),
   112→  getAuthToken: (): Promise<string> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:getAuthToken"),
   113→
   114→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→  // Native OS operations
   116→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   117→
   118→  pickFolder: (): Promise<string | null> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:pickFolder"),
   119→  confirm: (message: string, detail?: string): Promise<boolean> =>
   120→    ipcRenderer.invoke("native:confirm", { message, detail }),
   121→  setTheme: (theme: "light" | "dark" | "system"): Promise<void> =>
   122→    ipcRenderer.invoke("native:setTheme", { theme }),
   123→  openExternal: (url: string): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:openExternal", { url }),
   124→  contextMenu: (
   125→    items: Array<{ id: string; label: string; type?: string; enabled?: boolean }>
   126→  ): Promise<string | null> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:contextMenu", { items }),
   127→
   128→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   129→  // Window visibility & control
   130→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→
   132→  showMainWindow: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:showMainWindow"),
   133→  enterOnboardingMode: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:enterOnboardingMode"),
   134→  exitOnboardingMode: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:exitOnboardingMode"),
   135→  minimize: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:minimize"),
   136→  maximize: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:maximize"),
   137→  close: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:close"),
   138→  isMaximized: (): Promise<boolean> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:isMaximized"),
   139→  isFullscreen: (): Promise<boolean> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:isFullscreen"),
   140→  toggleFullscreen: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:toggleFullscreen"),
   141→
   142→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   143→  // Browser views (for agent browser automation)
   144→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   145→
   146→  browserInvoke: (method: string, args: unknown): Promise<unknown> =>
   147→    ipcRenderer.invoke(`browser:${method}`, args),
   148→  onBrowserEvent: (event: string, callback: (...args: unknown[]) => void): (() => void) => {
   149→    const listener = (_e: Electron.IpcRendererEvent, ...args: unknown[]): void => callback(...args);
   150→    ipcRenderer.on(event, listener);
   151→    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener(event, listener);
   152→  },
   153→
   154→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   155→  // Auto-update
   156→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   157→
   158→  checkForUpdates: (): Promise<unknown> => ipcRenderer.invoke("update:check"),
   159→  downloadUpdate: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("update:download"),
   160→  installUpdate: (): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke("update:install"),
   161→  getUpdateState: (): Promise<unknown> => ipcRenderer.invoke("update:getState"),
   162→  onUpdateState: (callback: (state: unknown) => void): (() => void) => {
   163→    const listener = (_e: Electron.IpcRendererEvent, state: unknown): void => callback(state);
   164→    ipcRenderer.on("update:state", listener);
   165→    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener("update:state", listener);
   166→  },
   167→
   168→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   169→  // CLI / Environment checks
   170→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   171→
   172→  checkCliTool: (tool: string): Promise<{ installed: boolean; path: string | null }> =>
   173→    ipcRenderer.invoke("native:checkCliTool", { tool }),
   174→  checkGhAuth: (): Promise<{ authenticated: boolean }> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:checkGhAuth"),
   175→  getInstalledApps: (): Promise<Array<{ name: string; path: string }>> =>
   176→    ipcRenderer.invoke("native:getInstalledApps"),
   177→  openInApp: (appPath: string, filePath: string): Promise<boolean> =>
   178→    ipcRenderer.invoke("native:openInApp", { appPath, filePath }),
   179→
   180→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   181→  // App info
   182→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   183→
   184→  getAppVersion: (): Promise<string> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:getAppVersion"),
   185→  getPlatform: (): Promise<string> => ipcRenderer.invoke("native:getPlatform"),
   186→
   187→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   188→  // Generic IPC bridge — guarded by allowlist to prevent the renderer from
   189→  // calling arbitrary ipcMain handlers. Only channels listed in
   190→  // ALLOWED_INVOKE_CHANNELS / ALLOWED_EVENT_CHANNELS are permitted.
   191→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→
   193→  invoke: (channel: string, args?: unknown): Promise<unknown> => {
   194→    if (!ALLOWED_INVOKE_CHANNELS.has(channel)) {
   195→      return Promise.reject(new Error(`IPC invoke channel "${channel}" is not allowed`));
   196→    }
   197→    return ipcRenderer.invoke(channel, args);
   198→  },
   199→  on: (event: string, callback: (...args: unknown[]) => void): (() => void) => {
   200→    if (!ALLOWED_EVENT_CHANNELS.has(event)) {
   201→      console.warn(`[preload] Event channel "${event}" is not allowed`);
   202→      return () => {};
   203→    }
   204→    const listener = (_e: Electron.IpcRendererEvent, ...args: unknown[]): void => callback(...args);
   205→    ipcRenderer.on(event, listener);
   206→    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener(event, listener);
   207→  },
   208→  send: (channel: string, ...args: unknown[]): void => {
   209→    if (!ALLOWED_EVENT_CHANNELS.has(channel)) {
   210→      console.warn(`[preload] Send channel "${channel}" is not allowed`);
   211→      return;
   212→    }
   213→    ipcRenderer.send(channel, ...args);
   214→  },
   215→
   216→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   217→  // Backend lifecycle (main process sends this after backend crash + restart)
   218→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   219→
   220→  onBackendPortChanged: (callback: (payload: { port: number }) => void): (() => void) => {
   221→    const listener = (_e: Electron.IpcRendererEvent, payload: { port: number }): void =>
   222→      callback(payload);
   223→    ipcRenderer.on("backend:port-changed", listener);
   224→    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener("backend:port-changed", listener);
   225→  },
   226→
   227→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   228→  // Fullscreen state (main process sends this on enter/leave-full-screen)
   229→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   230→
   231→  onFullscreenChange: (callback: (payload: { isFullscreen: boolean }) => void): (() => void) => {
   232→    const listener = (_e: Electron.IpcRendererEvent, payload: { isFullscreen: boolean }): void =>
   233→      callback(payload);
   234→    ipcRenderer.on("fullscreen-change", listener);
   235→    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener("fullscreen-change", listener);
   236→  },
   237→};
   238→
   239→contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld("electronAPI", electronAPI);
   240→

content.text:
Clean build. Here's what was done:

### Changes

**1. App Menu** (`apps/desktop/main/app-menu.ts`) — New file
- Proper macOS app menu with "Deus" as the name (no more "Electron" or generic labels)
- Standard **Edit** menu with undo/redo/copy/paste/select all keyboard shortcuts
- **View** menu with zoom, reload, devtools, fullscreen
- **Window** menu with minimize/zoom
- **Help** menu with docs link, issue reporting, devtools toggle

**2. System Tray** (`apps/desktop/main/tray.ts`) — New file
- Adds a Deus icon next to the clock (uses the app icon as a macOS template image)
- Polls `GET /api/health` every 10s to show backend status:
  - 🟢 Running — backend healthy
  - 🔴 Offline — backend unreachable
  - 🟡 Starting — initial boot
- Context menu: status indicator, "Show Window", "Quit Deus"
- Click tray icon → show/focus the main window

**3. Wired into `index.ts`**
- App menu set up immediately on `app.whenReady()`
- Tray created after backend spawns (so it knows the port)
- Tray destroyed on `before-quit`

{
  "name": "deus",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "AI-powered IDE for managing multiple parallel coding agents",
  "author": {
    "name": "Deus",
    "email": "hello@rundeus.com"
  },
  "repository": "github:zvadaadam/deus",
  "packageManager": "bun@1.2.19",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "([ -f apps/agent-server/dist/index.bundled.cjs ] || bun run build:agent-server) && electron-vite dev",
    "dev:web": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "dev:frontend": "vite --config apps/web/vite.config.ts",
    "dev:backend": "node apps/backend/server.cjs",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "build:agent-server": "bunx tsx apps/agent-server/build.ts && bun install --frozen-lockfile --cwd packages/mcp-notebook && bunx tsx packages/mcp-notebook/build.ts",
    "build:backend": "bunx tsx apps/backend/build.ts",
    "dev:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler dev",
    "build:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy --dry-run --outdir dist",
    "deploy:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy",
    "build:web": "vite build --config apps/web/vite.config.ts --outDir dist/web",
    "build:all": "bun run build:agent-server && bun run build:backend && bun run build",
    "package:mac": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --mac",
    "package:win": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --win",
    "package:linux": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --linux",
    "postinstall": "electron-builder install-app-deps",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator": "vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator:watch": "vitest --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:e2e": "node test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs",
    "test:backend": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:backend:watch": "vitest --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server:unit": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts --exclude '**/e2e.test.ts'",
    "test:agent-server:e2e": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts test/e2e.test.ts",
    "test:agent-server:watch": "vitest --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc -b",
    "typecheck:backend": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/backend/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:agent-server": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/agent-server/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:mcp-notebook": "tsc --noEmit --project packages/mcp-notebook/tsconfig.json",
    "test:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test",
    "test:relay:watch": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test:watch",
    "typecheck:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && tsc --noEmit",
    "typecheck:all": "bun run typecheck && bun run typecheck:backend && bun run typecheck:agent-server && bun run typecheck:mcp-notebook && bun run typecheck:relay",
    "format": "prettier --write \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "lint": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx",
    "lint:fix": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx --fix",
    "prepare": "husky",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6006",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk": "^0.2.63",
    "@codemirror/lang-javascript": "^6.2.2",
    "@codemirror/lang-python": "^6.1.6",
    "@codemirror/state": "^6.4.1",
    "@codemirror/view": "^6.33.0",
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@hapi/boom": "^10.0.1",
    "@hono/node-server": "^1.19.9",
    "@hono/node-ws": "^1.3.0",
    "@number-flow/react": "^0.5.12",
    "@openai/codex-sdk": "^0.101.0",
    "@pierre/diffs": "^1.0.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-avatar": "^1.1.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-checkbox": "^1.3.3",
    "@radix-ui/react-collapsible": "^1.1.12",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu": "^2.1.16",
    "@radix-ui/react-label": "^2.1.7",
    "@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu": "^1.2.14",
    "@radix-ui/react-popover": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area": "^1.2.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.2.6",
    "@radix-ui/react-separator": "^1.1.7",
    "@radix-ui/react-slot": "^1.2.3",
    "@radix-ui/react-switch": "^1.2.6",
    "@radix-ui/react-tabs": "^1.1.13",
    "@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "^1.2.8",
    "@sentry/node": "^10.40.0",
    "@sentry/react": "^10.40.0",
    "@sentry/vite-plugin": "^5.1.1",
    "@tanstack/react-query": "^5.90.5",
    "@tanstack/react-query-devtools": "^5.90.2",
    "@tanstack/react-router": "^1.168.1",
    "@tanstack/react-virtual": "^3.13.19",
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.11.0",
    "@xterm/addon-web-links": "^0.12.0",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
    "agent-browser": "^0.21.4",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.4.1",
    "chokidar": "^4.0.0",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "cmdk": "^1.1.1",
    "electron-updater": "^6.0.0",
    "framer-motion": "^12.23.24",
    "hono": "^4.11.7",
    "immer": "^11.0.1",
    "json-rpc-2.0": "^1.7.1",
    "lucide-react": "^0.546.0",
    "mermaid": "^11.4.1",
    "next-themes": "^0.4.6",
    "node-pty": "^1.0.0",
    "posthog-js": "^1.356.1",
    "qrcode.react": "^4.2.0",
    "react": "^18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "^18.3.1",
    "react-error-boundary": "^6.0.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-resizable-panels": "^3.0.6",
    "rehype-raw": "^7.0.0",
    "rehype-sanitize": "^6.0.0",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "shiki": "^3.14.0",
    "sonner": "^2.0.7",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.3.1",
    "ts-pattern": "^5.9.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "ws": "^8.19.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@chromatic-com/storybook": "^4.1.3",
    "@electron-toolkit/tsconfig": "^2.0.0",
    "@electron-toolkit/utils": "^4.0.0",
    "@electron/rebuild": "^4.0.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@storybook/addon-a11y": "^10.1.10",
    "@storybook/addon-docs": "^10.1.10",
    "@storybook/addon-onboarding": "^10.1.10",
    "@storybook/addon-vitest": "^10.1.10",
    "@storybook/react-vite": "^10.1.10",
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^18.3.3",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.3.0",
    "@types/ws": "^8.18.1",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.1",
    "@vitest/browser-playwright": "^4.0.16",
    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0.16",
    "electron": "^35.0.0",
    "electron-builder": "^26.0.0",
    "electron-vite": "^3.0.0",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "eslint-plugin-react": "^7.37.5",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.0.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.25",
    "eslint-plugin-storybook": "^10.1.10",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "husky": "^9.1.7",
    "lint-staged": "^16.2.7",
    "playwright": "^1.57.0",
    "prettier": "^3.6.2",
    "prettier-plugin-tailwindcss": "^0.7.1",
    "storybook": "^10.1.10",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.50.0",
    "vite": "^5.4.1",
    "vite-plugin-svgr": "^4.5.0",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16",
    "yallist": "4.0.0"
  },
  "prettier": {
    "plugins": [
      "prettier-plugin-tailwindcss"
    ],
    "semi": true,
    "singleQuote": false,
    "tabWidth": 2,
    "trailingComma": "es5",
    "printWidth": 100,
    "endOfLine": "lf"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{json,md,html,css}": [
      "prettier --write"
    ]
  }
}

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the audit findings.

## Package.json Dependency Audit Report

Based on thorough searching of the codebase (excluding worktree directories like `.opendevs`, `.conductor`, `.hive`), here are my findings:

### DEPENDENCIES - ACTUAL USAGE:

**USED:**
1. **@dnd-kit/core** - USED
   - Files: `src/features/workspace/ui/MainContentTabs.tsx`, `src/features/sidebar/ui/AppSidebar.tsx`
   - Usage: Tab dragging and repository reordering

2. **@dnd-kit/sortable** - USED
   - Files: `src/features/workspace/ui/SortableTab.tsx`, `src/features/sidebar/ui/DraggableRepository.tsx`
   - Usage: Sortable drag-and-drop for tabs and sidebar items

3. **@dnd-kit/utilities** - USED
   - Files: `src/features/workspace/ui/SortableTab.tsx`, `src/features/sidebar/ui/DraggableRepository.tsx`
   - Usage: Supporting utilities for dnd-kit functionality

4. **@number-flow/react** - USED
   - Files: `src/features/workspace/ui/CodePanelContent.tsx`, plus multiple other workspace features
   - Usage: Animated number displays for file change counts

5. **@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu** - DEFINED BUT UNUSED
   - File: `src/components/ui/navigation-menu.tsx` (shadcn base component wrapper)
   - Status: Component exists but not imported anywhere in the codebase
   - Can be removed: **YES**

6. **@radix-ui/react-checkbox** - USED
   - File: `src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx` (shadcn wrapper) - used throughout UI
   - Status: Component wrapper exists and is imported by the shadcn component

7. **@pierre/diffs** - USED
   - File: `src/features/workspace/ui/DiffViewer.tsx`
   - Usage: Git diff rendering with syntax highlighting

8. **mermaid** - USED
   - Files: `src/components/markdown/MermaidDiagram.tsx`
   - Usage: Rendering mermaid diagrams in markdown content

9. **cmdk** - USED
   - File: `src/components/ui/command.tsx` (shadcn wrapper)
   - Usage: Command palette/command menu component

10. **rehype-raw** - USED
    - File: `src/components/markdown/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`
    - Usage: Raw HTML rendering in markdown

11. **rehype-sanitize** - USED
    - File: `src/components/markdown/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`
    - Usage: HTML sanitization for security

12. **immer** - USED
    - Files: `src/features/workspace/api/workspace.queries.ts`, `src/features/settings/api/settings.queries.ts`, `src/features/session/api/session.queries.ts`
    - Usage: Immutable state updates with React Query

13. **react-error-boundary** - USED
    - Files: Multiple error fallback components in `src/shared/components/error-fallbacks/`
    - Usage: Error boundary wrapping for error handling

14. **qrcode.react** - LIKELY UNUSED (partial usage found)
    - File: `src/features/settings/ui/sections/AccessSection.tsx` (only in older worktrees)
    - Status: Not found in main source files, only in archived worktrees

15. **@types/better-sqlite3** - UNUSED
    - Status: No imports found in main source
    - Note: `better-sqlite3` is not used in this codebase (was from older architecture)
    - Can be removed: **YES**

**UNUSED (Not found in codebase):**
- **@codemirror/lang-javascript** - UNUSED
- **@codemirror/lang-python** - UNUSED  
- **@codemirror/state** - UNUSED
- **@codemirror/view** - UNUSED
- **@hapi/boom** - UNUSED
- **agent-browser** - UNUSED
- **next-themes** - UNUSED (though it's defined in package.json, never imported)

---

### DEVDEPENDENCIES - ACTUAL USAGE:

**USED:**
1. **@chromatic-com/storybook** - DEFINED
   - Package.json includes it (for Storybook visual regression testing)

2. **@storybook/addon-onboarding** - DEFINED
   - Package.json includes it, but `.storybook/main.ts` has empty `addons: []`
   - Status: Installed but disabled in config
   - Can be removed: **YES**

3. **@vitest/browser-playwright** - DEFINED
   - Package.json includes it but vitest configs use `environment: 'node'`
   - Status: Installed but not used in any vitest config
   - Can be removed: **YES**

4. **@vitest/coverage-v8** - DEFINED
   - Package.json includes it but not referenced in any vitest config
   - Status: Installed but not configured/used
   - Can be removed: **YES**

5. **vite-plugin-svgr** - DEFINED
   - Package.json includes it, but not found in `vite.config.ts` or electron configs
   - Status: Installed but not used
   - Can be removed: **YES**

6. **yallist** - LIKELY TRANSITIVE DEPENDENCY
   - Not directly imported; likely pulled in by another dependency
   - Can be removed: **Probably** (will be reinstalled as transitive if needed)

7. **@electron/rebuild** - DEFINED BUT LIKELY UNUSED
   - Package.json includes it but not referenced in build/scripts
   - Status: Not found in use
   - Can be removed: **YES**

---

### SUMMARY OF REMOVABLE DEPENDENCIES:

**Definite Removals (not used anywhere):**
- @codemirror/lang-javascript
- @codemirror/lang-python
- @codemirror/state
- @codemirror/view
- @hapi/boom
- agent-browser
- @types/better-sqlite3
- @storybook/addon-onboarding
- @vitest/browser-playwright
- @vitest/coverage-v8
- vite-plugin-svgr
- @electron/rebuild

**Conditional Removals (shadcn components that exist but aren't used):**
- @radix-ui/react-navigation-menu (the shadcn wrapper exists but is never imported)

**Check Before Removing:**
- qrcode.react (may have been used in settings but not in main source - verify if needed)
- yallist (transitive dependency - check if safe to remove)

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Here's my deep review:

---

## Package.json Deep Review

### Metadata Issues

- `"repository": "github:zvadaadam/deus"` — the actual repo is `zvadaadam/box-ide`. This is wrong and will confuse `npm bugs`, `electron-builder` publish links, etc.

### Scripts

| Script | Issue |
|---|---|
| `dev:backend` | Runs `node apps/backend/server.cjs` — does this file exist? Backend should be started via `dev:web` (dev.sh). This is potentially dead. |
| `preview` | `vite preview` — no config path specified, probably doesn't work |
| `lint` | `eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx` — ESLint 9 flat config ignores `--ext`. This flag does nothing. |
| `lint:fix` | Same issue |

### Dependencies — Remove These (0 imports found)

| Package | Why it's dead |
|---|---|
| `@codemirror/lang-javascript` | Zero imports. CodeMirror was removed. |
| `@codemirror/lang-python` | Same |
| `@codemirror/state` | Same |
| `@codemirror/view` | Same |
| `@hapi/boom` | Zero imports. Backend uses Hono's error handling. |
| `agent-browser` | Zero imports. Browser automation uses `BrowserView` now. |

### Dependencies — Keep But Watch

| Package | Status |
|---|---|
| `next-themes` | Used by `sonner.tsx` (toast theming). Keep — it's a real import. |
| `qrcode.react` | Used in `AccessSection.tsx` for device pairing QR codes. Keep. |
| `@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu` | Component file exists (`navigation-menu.tsx`) but **nothing imports it**. Dead shadcn component. Remove both the file and the dep. |
| `@radix-ui/react-checkbox` | Component file exists (`checkbox.tsx`) but **nothing imports it**. Same situation. Remove both. |
| `@types/better-sqlite3` | Should be in **devDependencies**, not dependencies. `better-sqlite3` is used by the backend. |
| `vite-plugin-svgr` | Used in both `electron.vite.config.ts` and `apps/web/vite.config.ts`. Keep — the agent was wrong. |

### DevDependencies — Remove These

| Package | Why |
|---|---|
| `@chromatic-com/storybook` | Not in `.storybook/main.ts` addons. Unused. |
| `@storybook/addon-onboarding` | Not in addons list (addons is `[]`). Dead. |
| `@storybook/addon-vitest` | Not in addons. Dead. |
| `@storybook/addon-a11y` | Not in addons. Dead. |
| `@storybook/addon-docs` | Not in addons. Dead. |
| `eslint-plugin-storybook` | Not referenced in `eslint.config.mjs`. Dead. |
| `@vitest/browser-playwright` | No vitest config uses browser mode. Dead. |
| `@vitest/coverage-v8` | No vitest config has coverage enabled. Dead. |
| `@electron/rebuild` | `postinstall` uses `electron-builder install-app-deps` (not `@electron/rebuild`). They do the same thing — `electron-builder install-app-deps` doesn't need this package. Can remove. |
| `yallist` | Pinned at `4.0.0` — likely a hoisted resolution hack. Check if still needed. |

### Dependencies Misplaced

| Package | Currently | Should Be |
|---|---|---|
| `@types/better-sqlite3` | dependencies | devDependencies |
| `tsx` | dependencies | devDependencies (build-time tool, not runtime) |

### Summary

**6 dead dependencies** to remove from `dependencies`:
- `@codemirror/lang-javascript`, `@codemirror/lang-python`, `@codemirror/state`, `@codemirror/view`
- `@hapi/boom`
- `agent-browser`

**2 dead shadcn components** (remove package + component file):
- `@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu`
- `@radix-ui/react-checkbox`

**7 dead devDependencies** to remove:
- `@chromatic-com/storybook`, `@storybook/addon-onboarding`, `@storybook/addon-vitest`, `@storybook/addon-a11y`, `@storybook/addon-docs`, `eslint-plugin-storybook`
- `@vitest/browser-playwright`, `@vitest/coverage-v8`
- `@electron/rebuild`

**2 to move** from dependencies → devDependencies:
- `@types/better-sqlite3`
- `tsx`

Want me to make these changes?

content.thinking:
`tsx` is used via `bunx tsx` in build scripts. Since it's used via `bunx`, it doesn't actually need to be installed as a dependency — `bunx` will use it from the local node_modules if present, or download it. But since it's in the scripts, it's safer to keep it installed. However, it should be in devDependencies, not dependencies, since it's only used for building.

Wait, actually `bunx tsx` will use the locally installed version. If `tsx` is moved to devDependencies, it would still work since devDependencies are installed during development. 

In CI, `bun install --frozen-lockfile` installs everything including devDependencies, so `tsx` would be available there too. The key insight is that `bunx tsx` resolves from node_modules regardless of whether it's in dependencies or devDependencies — both get installed locally and in CI. Since `tsx` is only needed for build scripts and not at runtime in the packaged Electron app, it belongs in devDependencies, and `electron-builder` won't include it in the final bundle anyway.

Now I need to double-check the `@electron/rebuild` situation. The `postinstall` script runs `electron-builder install-app-deps`, which is electron-builder's native module rebuilder, but I'm not certain whether it internally depends on `@electron/rebuild` or if that's a separate concern. Older versions used `electron-rebuild`, but newer versions of electron-builder might handle this differently, so removing `@electron/rebuild` could potentially break the postinstall script if it's still needed.

{
  "name": "deus",

  "packageManager": "bun@1.2.19",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "([ -f apps/agent-server/dist/index.bundled.cjs ] || bun run build:agent-server) && electron-vite dev",
    "dev:web": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "dev:frontend": "vite --config apps/web/vite.config.ts",
    "dev:backend": "node apps/backend/server.cjs",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "build:agent-server": "bunx tsx apps/agent-server/build.ts && bun install --frozen-lockfile --cwd packages/mcp-notebook && bunx tsx packages/mcp-notebook/build.ts",
    "build:backend": "bunx tsx apps/backend/build.ts",
    "dev:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler dev",
    "build:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy --dry-run --outdir dist",
    "deploy:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy",
    "build:web": "vite build --config apps/web/vite.config.ts --outDir dist/web",
    "build:all": "bun run build:agent-server && bun run build:backend && bun run build",
    "package:mac": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --mac",
    "package:win": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --win",
    "package:linux": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --linux",
    "postinstall": "electron-builder install-app-deps",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator": "vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator:watch": "vitest --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:e2e": "node test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs",
    "test:backend": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:backend:watch": "vitest --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",

    "test:agent-server:watch": "vitest --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc -b",

    "typecheck:agent-server": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/agent-server/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:mcp-notebook": "tsc --noEmit --project packages/mcp-notebook/tsconfig.json",
    "test:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test",
    "test:relay:watch": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test:watch",
    "typecheck:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && tsc --noEmit",
    "typecheck:all": "bun run typecheck && bun run typecheck:backend && bun run typecheck:agent-server && bun run typecheck:mcp-notebook && bun run typecheck:relay",
    "format": "prettier --write \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "lint": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx",
    "lint:fix": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx --fix",
    "prepare": "husky",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6006",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build"
  },

  "type": "module",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "([ -f apps/agent-server/dist/index.bundled.cjs ] || bun run build:agent-server) && electron-vite dev",
    "dev:web": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "dev:frontend": "vite --config apps/web/vite.config.ts",
    "dev:backend": "node apps/backend/server.cjs",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "build:agent-server": "bunx tsx apps/agent-server/build.ts && bun install --frozen-lockfile --cwd packages/mcp-notebook && bunx tsx packages/mcp-notebook/build.ts",
    "build:backend": "bunx tsx apps/backend/build.ts",
    "dev:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler dev",
    "build:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy --dry-run --outdir dist",
    "deploy:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy",
    "build:web": "vite build --config apps/web/vite.config.ts --outDir dist/web",
    "build:all": "bun run build:agent-server && bun run build:backend && bun run build",
    "package:mac": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --mac",
    "package:win": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --win",
    "package:linux": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --linux",
    "postinstall": "electron-builder install-app-deps",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator": "vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator:watch": "vitest --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:e2e": "node test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs",
    "test:backend": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:backend:watch": "vitest --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server:unit": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts --exclude '**/e2e.test.ts'",
    "test:agent-server:e2e": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts test/e2e.test.ts",
    "test:agent-server:watch": "vitest --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc -b",
    "typecheck:backend": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/backend/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:agent-server": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/agent-server/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:mcp-notebook": "tsc --noEmit --project packages/mcp-notebook/tsconfig.json",
    "test:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test",
    "test:relay:watch": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test:watch",
    "typecheck:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && tsc --noEmit",
    "typecheck:all": "bun run typecheck && bun run typecheck:backend && bun run typecheck:agent-server && bun run typecheck:mcp-notebook && bun run typecheck:relay",
    "format": "prettier --write \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "lint": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx",

    "prepare": "husky",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6006",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build"
  },

{
  "name": "deus",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "AI-powered IDE for managing multiple parallel coding agents",
  "author": {
    "name": "Deus",
    "email": "hello@rundeus.com"
  },
  "repository": "github:zvadaadam/box-ide",
  "packageManager": "bun@1.2.19",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "([ -f apps/agent-server/dist/index.bundled.cjs ] || bun run build:agent-server) && electron-vite dev",
    "dev:web": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "dev:frontend": "vite --config apps/web/vite.config.ts",
    "dev:backend": "node apps/backend/server.cjs",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "build:agent-server": "bunx tsx apps/agent-server/build.ts && bun install --frozen-lockfile --cwd packages/mcp-notebook && bunx tsx packages/mcp-notebook/build.ts",
    "build:backend": "bunx tsx apps/backend/build.ts",
    "dev:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler dev",
    "build:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy --dry-run --outdir dist",
    "deploy:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && wrangler deploy",
    "build:web": "vite build --config apps/web/vite.config.ts --outDir dist/web",
    "build:all": "bun run build:agent-server && bun run build:backend && bun run build",
    "package:mac": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --mac",
    "package:win": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --win",
    "package:linux": "bun run build:all && electron-builder --linux",
    "postinstall": "electron-builder install-app-deps",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts && vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator": "vitest run --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:simulator:watch": "vitest --config test/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:e2e": "node test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs",
    "test:backend": "vitest run --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:backend:watch": "vitest --config apps/backend/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:agent-server:unit": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts --exclude '**/e2e.test.ts'",
    "test:agent-server:e2e": "vitest run --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts test/e2e.test.ts",
    "test:agent-server:watch": "vitest --config apps/agent-server/vitest.config.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc -b",
    "typecheck:backend": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/backend/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:agent-server": "tsc --noEmit --project apps/agent-server/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:mcp-notebook": "tsc --noEmit --project packages/mcp-notebook/tsconfig.json",
    "test:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test",
    "test:relay:watch": "cd apps/cloud-relay && bun run test:watch",
    "typecheck:relay": "cd apps/cloud-relay && tsc --noEmit",
    "typecheck:all": "bun run typecheck && bun run typecheck:backend && bun run typecheck:agent-server && bun run typecheck:mcp-notebook && bun run typecheck:relay",
    "format": "prettier --write \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"apps/**/*.{ts,tsx,css,json}\"",
    "lint": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx",
    "lint:fix": "eslint apps --ext .ts,.tsx --fix",
    "prepare": "husky",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6006",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk": "^0.2.63",
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@hono/node-server": "^1.19.9",
    "@hono/node-ws": "^1.3.0",
    "@number-flow/react": "^0.5.12",
    "@openai/codex-sdk": "^0.101.0",
    "@pierre/diffs": "^1.0.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-avatar": "^1.1.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-collapsible": "^1.1.12",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu": "^2.1.16",
    "@radix-ui/react-label": "^2.1.7",
    "@radix-ui/react-popover": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area": "^1.2.10",
    "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.2.6",
    "@radix-ui/react-separator": "^1.1.7",
    "@radix-ui/react-slot": "^1.2.3",
    "@radix-ui/react-switch": "^1.2.6",
    "@radix-ui/react-tabs": "^1.1.13",
    "@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "^1.2.8",
    "@sentry/node": "^10.40.0",
    "@sentry/react": "^10.40.0",
    "@sentry/vite-plugin": "^5.1.1",
    "@tanstack/react-query": "^5.90.5",
    "@tanstack/react-query-devtools": "^5.90.2",
    "@tanstack/react-router": "^1.168.1",
    "@tanstack/react-virtual": "^3.13.19",
    "@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.11.0",
    "@xterm/addon-web-links": "^0.12.0",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
    "agent-browser": "^0.21.4",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.4.1",
    "chokidar": "^4.0.0",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "cmdk": "^1.1.1",
    "electron-updater": "^6.0.0",
    "framer-motion": "^12.23.24",
    "hono": "^4.11.7",
    "immer": "^11.0.1",
    "json-rpc-2.0": "^1.7.1",
    "lucide-react": "^0.546.0",
    "mermaid": "^11.4.1",
    "next-themes": "^0.4.6",
    "node-pty": "^1.0.0",
    "posthog-js": "^1.356.1",
    "qrcode.react": "^4.2.0",
    "react": "^18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "^18.3.1",
    "react-error-boundary": "^6.0.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-resizable-panels": "^3.0.6",
    "rehype-raw": "^7.0.0",
    "rehype-sanitize": "^6.0.0",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "shiki": "^3.14.0",
    "sonner": "^2.0.7",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.3.1",
    "ts-pattern": "^5.9.0",
    "ws": "^8.19.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@electron-toolkit/tsconfig": "^2.0.0",
    "@electron-toolkit/utils": "^4.0.0",
    "@electron/rebuild": "^4.0.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@storybook/react-vite": "^10.1.10",
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^18.3.3",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.3.0",
    "@types/ws": "^8.18.1",
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.1",
    "electron": "^35.0.0",
    "electron-builder": "^26.0.0",
    "electron-vite": "^3.0.0",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "eslint-plugin-react": "^7.37.5",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.0.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.25",
    "eslint-plugin-storybook": "^10.1.10",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "husky": "^9.1.7",
    "lint-staged": "^16.2.7",
    "playwright": "^1.57.0",
    "prettier": "^3.6.2",
    "prettier-plugin-tailwindcss": "^0.7.1",
    "storybook": "^10.1.10",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.50.0",
    "vite": "^5.4.1",
    "vite-plugin-svgr": "^4.5.0",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16",
    "yallist": "4.0.0"
  },
  "prettier": {
    "plugins": [
      "prettier-plugin-tailwindcss"
    ],
    "semi": true,
    "singleQuote": false,
    "tabWidth": 2,
    "trailingComma": "es5",
    "printWidth": 100,
    "endOfLine": "lf"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{json,md,html,css}": [
      "prettier --write"
    ]
  }
}

Clean install, 16 packages removed. And note — `@electron/rebuild` was confirmed used during the postinstall step (`executing @electron/rebuild`). Good thing we kept it.

Let me verify the typecheck still passes:

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
All clean. Here's a summary of changes:

### Removed from dependencies (6 packages)
- `@codemirror/lang-javascript`, `@codemirror/lang-python`, `@codemirror/state`, `@codemirror/view` — CodeMirror was removed, zero imports
- `@hapi/boom` — unused, backend uses Hono's error handling
- `@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu`, `@radix-ui/react-checkbox` — dead shadcn components (wrapper files deleted too)

### Removed from devDependencies (7 packages)
- `@chromatic-com/storybook`, `@storybook/addon-a11y`, `@storybook/addon-docs`, `@storybook/addon-onboarding`, `@storybook/addon-vitest` — none in storybook addons config
- `@vitest/browser-playwright`, `@vitest/coverage-v8` — no vitest config uses them

### Moved to devDependencies (2 packages)
- `@types/better-sqlite3` — types are build-time only
- `tsx` — only used in build scripts via `bunx tsx`

### Fixed
- `repository` field: `github:zvadaadam/deus` → `github:zvadaadam/box-ide`

**16 packages removed** from `node_modules`.

content.text: This branch has 106 commits with a huge scope. Let me create the PR.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "feat: rename sidecar to agent-server, app menu, tray, dep cleanup" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

This branch contains the full development history (106 commits) from the initial codebase through the current state. The latest changes on top include:

- **Rename `apps/sidecar` → `apps/agent-server`** across the entire codebase — imports, configs, docs, scripts, and CI workflows all updated to reflect the new name
- **Native app menu** with proper "Deus" branding, standard Edit/View/Window/Help menus with keyboard shortcuts
- **System tray icon** next to the clock showing backend health status (green/red/yellow dot) with context menu
- **Dev dock icon** (orange dot variant) to visually distinguish dev builds from production in the macOS dock
- **Dependency cleanup** — removed 13 unused packages (CodeMirror, @hapi/boom, dead Storybook addons, unused Radix components), moved `@types/better-sqlite3` and `tsx` to devDependencies, deleted dead shadcn component files
- **Fix** repository field in package.json and eslint `setState-in-effect` warning in UpdateSection

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `bun run dev` — verify app menu shows "Deus" (not "Electron"), tray icon appears next to clock
- [ ] Verify tray shows green dot after backend starts, context menu works
- [ ] Run `bun run test:backend` and `bun run test:agent-server` — all tests pass
- [ ] Run `bun run typecheck` — clean build
- [ ] Verify `bun install` succeeds with the reduced dependency set

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1

input.prompt:
Verify and fix incorrect claims that agent-server does DB writes. The agent-server is stateless with no DB access — only the backend writes to the DB. Check and fix ALL of these files:

1. `.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md` line 25 — says "direct SQLite writes", should say "stateless runtime (no direct DB writes)"
2. `.claude/agents/deep-reviewer.md` line ~29 — table says agent-server owns "assistant DB writes", should say "tool orchestration" and "Must NOT do" should include "direct DB writes"
3. `.claude/agents/dev.md` line 18 — says "assistant message persistence (direct SQLite via better-sqlite3)", should say "canonical event emission. Stateless process with no DB access"
4. `.claude/agents/dev.md` line 77 — says indexes go in `backend/src/lib/schema.ts` and `agent-server/db/schema.ts`, should point to `shared/schema.ts` only
5. `.claude/skills/explore/SKILL.md` line 19 — says "RPC handler → agent → SDK → DB write → frontend notification", should show backend handling persistence
6. `.claude/skills/explore/SKILL.md` lines 40-43 — agent-server section shows "db/" directory for "Direct SQLite writes", should show "Canonical event emission → backend persists"
7. `.claude/skills/risk-tier/SKILL.md` line 130 — says "Schema changes require both backend and agent-server schema sync", should say "Schema changes require backend schema + query invalidation review (shared/schema.ts, query-engine, agent-event-handler)"

Read each file, verify the issue exists, and fix it. These are all documentation fixes — make each description accurate to the actual architecture.

Verify and fix: In `.claude/skills/risk-tier/SKILL.md`, the path globs for agent-server files are missing the `apps/` prefix. Files like `agent-server/index.ts` should be `apps/agent-server/index.ts` to match the monorepo structure.

Also check ALL other paths in that file — do backend paths need `apps/` prefix too? Check if paths like `backend/src/routes/**` should be `apps/backend/src/routes/**`. And check `src-tauri/` paths — those may no longer exist since we migrated to Electron.

Read the file, verify every path against the actual repo structure using Glob, and fix all incorrect paths.

Verify and fix three issues:

1. `apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line ~228 — Comment says "SQLITE_BUSY errors that can occur when the agent-server is concurrently accessing the DB." This is wrong — agent-server has NO DB access. Fix to say "concurrent backend operations".

2. `apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line ~270 — Comment says "SQLITE_BUSY can fire when agent-server holds the DB". Same problem. Fix to say "concurrent backend operations hold the DB".

3. `test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs` line ~124 — Uses `response.body.agent-server` which is INVALID JavaScript — the hyphen makes it `response.body.agent - server` (subtraction). This is a runtime bug. Check if the /health endpoint even returns an "agent-server" key (read `apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts`). If not, remove the line entirely. If it does, use bracket notation `response.body["agent-server"]`.

Read each file, verify the issues exist, and fix them.

content.text:
All three fixes are correct. Here is a summary of what was done and why:

**`apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line 228**
- Old: "errors that can occur when the agent-server is concurrently accessing the DB."
- New: "errors that can occur during concurrent backend operations."
- The agent-server is stateless and never touches the DB. The SQLITE_BUSY contention comes from other backend writes (e.g., the `updateInitStage` call at line 268 races with the transaction inside `finalize()`).

**`apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line 270**
- Old: "SQLITE_BUSY can fire when agent-server holds the DB"
- New: "SQLITE_BUSY can fire when concurrent backend operations hold the DB"
- Same reason as above.

**`test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs` line 124**
- Removed `logInfo(`Agent-server: ${response.body.agent-server}`);` entirely.
- Two problems: (1) `response.body.agent-server` is parsed as `response.body.agent - server` (subtraction of the identifier `server` from `response.body.agent`, both `undefined`, yielding `NaN`) — a silent runtime bug. (2) The `/health` endpoint in `apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts` does not return an `agent-server` field at all — the comment there explicitly states it was removed. The line was logging `NaN` or `undefined` and providing no value.

4. `.claude/agents/dev.md` line 77 — says indexes go in `backend/src/lib/schema.ts` and `agent-server/db/schema.ts`, should point to `shared/schema.ts` only
5. `.claude/skills/explore/SKILL.md` line 19 — says "RPC handler → agent → SDK → DB write → frontend notification", should show backend handling persistence
6. `.claude/skills/explore/SKILL.md` lines 40-43 — agent-server section shows "db/" directory for "Direct SQLite writes", should show "Canonical event emission → backend persists"
7. `.claude/skills/risk-tier/SKILL.md` line 130 — says "Schema changes require both backend and agent-server schema sync", should say "Schema changes require backend schema + query invalidation review (shared/schema.ts, query-engine, agent-event-handler)"
