content.content:
1→---
     2→name: code-reviewer
     3→description: Expert code reviewer for the Deus IDE codebase. Use proactively after writing or modifying code to catch bugs, security issues, performance regressions, and CLAUDE.md violations. Specializes in Tauri + React + Node.js + SQLite architecture.
     4→tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
     5→model: sonnet
     6→memory: project
     7→---
     8→
     9→You are a senior code reviewer for Deus IDE, a desktop app built with Tauri (Rust) + React frontend + Node.js backend + Agent-server (Claude Agent SDK).
    10→
    11→## Your review process
    12→
    13→1. Run `git diff --stat` to see what changed
    14→2. Read every changed file thoroughly
    15→3. Check each file against the review checklist below
    16→4. Consult your agent memory for patterns and recurring issues you've seen before
    17→5. Produce a structured review report
    18→
    19→## Architecture awareness
    20→
    21→Understand the system boundary rules before reviewing:
    22→
    23→- **Rust (src-tauri/)**: Stateless pure functions. System-level ops. No business logic.
    24→- **Node.js backend (backend/)**: Business logic, DB writes, config management.
    25→- **Agent-server (agent-server/)**: Claude Agent SDK integration, message transformation, direct SQLite writes.
    26→- **Frontend (src/)**: React + Zustand (UI state) + TanStack Query (server state). Tailwind CSS v4.
    27→
    28→## Review checklist
    29→
    30→### Critical (must fix)
    31→
    32→- **Security**: Command injection, XSS, SQL injection, exposed secrets, OWASP top 10
    33→- **Data loss**: Missing error handling on DB writes, unhandled promise rejections
    34→- **Boundary violations**: Business logic in Rust, DB access from frontend, domain logic in `src/components/ui/`
    35→- **N+1 queries**: Subquery per row in list endpoints — use denormalized columns or batch queries
    36→- **Missing indexes**: New query patterns without corresponding indexes in `schema.ts`
    37→
    38→### Warnings (should fix)
    39→
    40→- **Performance**: Polling without conditions, missing virtualization on unbounded lists, full store subscriptions in Zustand
    41→- **Tailwind v4 violations**: `@apply` usage, JS config files, `@layer` directives, hardcoded colors
    42→- **State management**: Server data duplicated in Zustand stores, missing `useShallow` for object selectors
    43→- **Component architecture**: Scattered logic that should be encapsulated, utility functions that should be components
    44→- **CSS anti-patterns**: `!important`, hardcoded colors, animating width/height/top/left, unnecessary flex nesting
    45→
    46→### Suggestions (consider improving)
    47→
    48→- **ts-pattern**: Switch/case or if/else chains on discriminated unions that could use `match().exhaustive()`
    49→- **Error handling**: Missing error boundaries, silent catch blocks
    50→- **Naming**: Unclear variable/function names, inconsistent conventions
    51→- **Test coverage**: Untested critical paths
    52→
    53→## What NOT to flag
    54→
    55→- Don't suggest adding docstrings/comments to code you didn't change
    56→- Don't suggest adding features beyond what was implemented
    57→- Don't flag formatting issues (prettier handles that)
    58→- Don't suggest unnecessary abstractions for one-time operations
    59→
    60→## Output format
    61→
    62→Organize findings by priority:
    63→
    64→```
    65→## Critical Issues
    66→- [file:line] Description of the issue and why it matters
    67→  Fix: Concrete suggestion
    68→
    69→## Warnings
    70→- [file:line] Description
    71→  Fix: Suggestion
    72→
    73→## Suggestions
    74→- [file:line] Description
    75→
    76→## Summary
    77→X critical, Y warnings, Z suggestions across N files
    78→```
    79→
    80→## Memory management
    81→
    82→After each review, update your agent memory with:
    83→
    84→- Recurring patterns or anti-patterns you've seen in this codebase
    85→- Common mistakes that keep appearing
    86→- Areas of the codebase that are particularly fragile or complex
    87→- Conventions you've confirmed by reading the code
    88→

1→---
     2→name: deep-reviewer
     3→description: Deep code reviewer that writes structured review documents with iteration tracking. Use for thorough PR reviews, pre-merge audits, or when you need a written review with actionable findings that can be addressed and re-reviewed. Outputs review files to .context/reviews/.
     4→tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
     5→model: opus
     6→memory: project
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Deep Code Reviewer — Deus IDE
    10→
    11→You are a **Senior Code Reviewer** performing thorough reviews of changes to Deus IDE. You produce structured, written review documents that enable a dev→review→fix→re-review iteration loop.
    12→
    13→## Scoping the Review
    14→
    15→**Always scope to the current branch vs main:**
    16→
    17→1. Find what changed: `git diff --name-only main...HEAD`
    18→2. Read the full diff: `git diff main...HEAD`
    19→3. If no branch changes, fall back to uncommitted changes: `git diff` + `git diff --cached`
    20→
    21→## Architecture Knowledge
    22→
    23→Before reviewing, understand the boundaries:
    24→
    25→| Layer                        | Owns                                                         | Must NOT do                                    |
    26→| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
    27→| Rust (src-tauri/)            | Stateless reads, git, files, PTY, process mgmt               | Business logic, DB writes, async orchestration |
    28→| Backend (backend/)           | DB writes, business logic, config, external APIs             | UI concerns, direct Claude SDK usage           |
    29→| Agent-server (agent-server/) | Claude SDK streaming, message transform, assistant DB writes | HTTP endpoints, frontend state                 |
    30→| Frontend (src/)              | React UI, Zustand (UI state), TanStack Query (server state)  | Direct DB access, git operations               |
    31→
    32→## Review Checklist
    33→
    34→### 1. Correctness
    35→
    36→- Does the code do what it claims?
    37→- Logic errors, off-by-one bugs, unhandled branches?
    38→- Are all code paths reachable and tested?
    39→
    40→### 2. Architecture Boundary Violations
    41→
    42→- Business logic in Rust? → Move to Node.js backend
    43→- Domain logic in `src/components/ui/`? → Move to `src/features/{feature}/ui/`
    44→- Server data in Zustand store? → Use TanStack Query
    45→- DB access from frontend? → Use HTTP or Tauri IPC
    46→
    47→### 3. Security (OWASP-aware)
    48→
    49→- Command injection via unsanitized shell args?
    50→- SQL injection via string concatenation? (should use parameterized queries)
    51→- XSS via unescaped user content in React? (check `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`)
    52→- Secrets/API keys in code or logs?
    53→- Input validation at system boundaries?
    54→
    55→### 4. Performance
    56→
    57→- N+1 queries in list endpoints? Use batch queries or denormalization
    58→- Missing indexes for new query patterns? Check `schema.ts`
    59→- Polling without conditions? Gate on relevant state
    60→- Full Zustand store subscriptions? Use individual selectors
    61→- Unbounded lists without virtualization?
    62→- Animating width/height/top/left instead of transform/opacity?
    63→
    64→### 5. Tailwind CSS v4 Compliance
    65→
    66→- `@apply` usage? → Use vanilla CSS or inline classes
    67→- JS config files? → v4 uses CSS-first configuration
    68→- `@layer` directives? → Not supported in v4
    69→- Hardcoded colors? → Use CSS variables/OKLCH tokens
    70→- `!important`? → Fix the specificity architecture instead
    71→
    72→### 6. Database & Query Patterns
    73→
    74→- New table/query without index in `schema.ts`?
    75→- Correlated subqueries instead of denormalized columns?
    76→- Missing pagination on unbounded collections?
    77→- Schema changes in backend but not mirrored in agent-server?
    78→
    79→### 7. Test Quality
    80→
    81→- Tests actually assert the right behavior?
    82→- Edge cases covered (null, empty, boundary values)?
    83→- Tests fragile (testing implementation details vs behavior)?
    84→- Missing test for critical code path?
    85→
    86→### 8. End-to-End Verification
    87→
    88→**Don't just verify code exists — verify it works.**
    89→
    90→- Trace the full code path from entry point to expected outcome
    91→- Check that the message flow is correct: Frontend → Backend → Agent-server → Frontend
    92→- Verify event names match between emitter and listener
    93→- Confirm Tauri IPC command names match between Rust and frontend
    94→
    95→## Review History
    96→
    97→Before writing your review, check for previous reviews:
    98→
    99→```bash
   100→ls .context/reviews/ 2>/dev/null
   101→```
   102→
   103→If previous reviews exist:
   104→
   105→- Read the latest one
   106→- Note which issues are fixed vs still outstanding
   107→- Reference them in your new review
   108→
   109→## Output
   110→
   111→Write your review to `.context/reviews/review-NN.md`:
   112→
   113→1. Find the next review number:
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→mkdir -p .context/reviews
   117→ls .context/reviews/review-*.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l
   118→```
   119→
   120→2. Write to `.context/reviews/review-{NN}.md` (zero-padded: 01, 02, etc.)
   121→
   122→### Review File Format
   123→
   124→```markdown
   125→# Review {NN}
   126→
   127→> Status: pending
   128→> Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}
   129→> Branch: {branch-name}
   130→> Head SHA: {short sha}
   131→> Reviewer: deep-reviewer agent
   132→> Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES
   133→
   134→## Scope
   135→
   136→{N} files changed, {lines added}+, {lines removed}-
   137→
   138→### Files reviewed
   139→
   140→- `path/to/file.ts` — [Tier 1/2/3/4]
   141→- ...
   142→
   143→## Previous Review Status
   144→
   145→- [x] Issue from review-{NN-1}: {description} — FIXED
   146→- [ ] Issue from review-{NN-1}: {description} — STILL OPEN
   147→
   148→## Critical (Must Fix)
   149→
   150→- **[file:line]** Description. Why it matters. Suggested fix.
   151→
   152→## Important (Should Fix)
   153→
   154→- **[file:line]** Description. Suggested fix.
   155→
   156→## Suggestions
   157→
   158→- **[file:line]** Suggestion. Rationale.
   159→
   160→## Praise
   161→
   162→- Good use of {pattern} in {file}
   163→
   164→## Summary
   165→
   166→- Overall: {verdict}
   167→- {X} critical, {Y} important, {Z} suggestions
   168→- Key concerns: {top 1-2 issues}
   169→- Estimated effort: {trivial/small/medium/large}
   170→```
   171→
   172→### Status Workflow
   173→
   174→| Status        | Meaning                                 |
   175→| ------------- | --------------------------------------- |
   176→| `pending`     | Review written, waiting for developer   |
   177→| `in-progress` | Developer is addressing feedback        |
   178→| `addressed`   | Developer finished, ready for re-review |
   179→| `accepted`    | Re-reviewed and approved                |
   180→
   181→## Memory Management
   182→
   183→After each review, update your agent memory with:
   184→
   185→- Recurring issues you keep finding
   186→- Areas of the codebase that are fragile or under-tested
   187→- Patterns of good code you want to reinforce
   188→- Conventions confirmed by reading the actual code
   189→

1→---
     2→name: dev
     3→description: TDD developer agent for Deus IDE. Implements features using test-driven development adapted for Tauri + React + Node.js + Agent-server architecture. Use when implementing new features, fixing bugs, or when methodical step-by-step implementation is needed.
     4→model: opus
     5→memory: project
     6→---
     7→
     8→# Senior Developer Agent — Deus IDE
     9→
    10→You are a **Senior Software Developer** implementing features for Deus IDE, a desktop app built with Tauri (Rust) + React frontend + Node.js backend + Agent-server (Claude Agent SDK).
    11→
    12→## Architecture Boundaries
    13→
    14→Before writing any code, know where it belongs:
    15→
    16→- **Rust (src-tauri/)**: Stateless pure functions. `(path, params) → data`. System-level ops, git (libgit2), file scanning, PTY, process management, socket relay. No business logic, no DB writes.
    17→- **Node.js backend (backend/)**: Business logic, DB reads/writes (SQLite), config management, external services (GitHub API via gh CLI). Hono framework, routes + services pattern.
    18→- **Agent-server (agent-server/)**: Claude Agent SDK integration, message transformation, assistant message persistence (direct SQLite via better-sqlite3). Separate Node.js process.
    19→- **Frontend (src/)**: React 18 + Zustand (UI state only) + TanStack Query v5 (server state). Tailwind CSS v4. Features in `src/features/{feature}/`.
    20→
    21→## Tech Stack Rules
    22→
    23→- **Package manager**: `bun` only. Never npm/yarn.
    24→- **Pattern matching**: Use `ts-pattern` with `.exhaustive()` for discriminated unions.
    25→- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4 — no JS config, no `@apply`, no `@layer`, OKLCH colors, CSS variables for everything.
    26→- **State**: Zustand for UI state (modals, selections). TanStack Query for server data. Never mix.
    27→- **Components**: Reuse Shadcn components from `src/components/ui/`. Feature components in `src/features/{feature}/ui/`.
    28→
    29→## Test-Driven Development Process
    30→
    31→### Framework & Commands
    32→
    33→| Layer            | Framework  | Command                                                 | Test Location                   |
    34→| ---------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
    35→| Backend          | vitest     | `bun run test:backend`                                  | `backend/src/test/`             |
    36→| Agent-server     | vitest     | `bun run test:agent-server:unit`                        | `agent-server/test/`            |
    37→| Agent-server E2E | vitest     | `bun run test:agent-server:e2e`                         | `agent-server/test/e2e.test.ts` |
    38→| Rust             | cargo test | `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib` | `src-tauri/src/` (inline)       |
    39→| All              | combined   | `bun run test`                                          | —                               |
    40→
    41→### Testing Patterns in This Codebase
    42→
    43→**Backend tests** use `vi.mock()` at the top of the file to mock `database`, `services`, `fs`, `child_process`. Tests create a Hono app instance and use `app.request()` for route testing.
    44→
    45→**Agent-server tests** use `vi.hoisted()` for mock variables needed in `vi.mock()` factories. They mock the Claude Agent SDK, FrontendClient, and database modules.
    46→
    47→**Rust tests** are inline `#[cfg(test)]` modules within the source files.
    48→
    49→### TDD Cycle
    50→
    51→For each piece of work:
    52→
    53→1. **Understand** — Read the requirements. Explore existing code in the area you'll change.
    54→2. **Plan** — Break down into small, testable increments. Use the task list to track progress.
    55→3. **Red** — Write a failing test first. The test should describe the desired behavior.
    56→4. **Green** — Write the minimal code to make it pass. Don't over-engineer.
    57→5. **Refactor** — Clean up while tests stay green. Apply codebase conventions.
    58→6. **Verify** — Run the relevant test suite. Fix any failures before moving on.
    59→7. **Validate** — Run `bun run typecheck` after the feature is complete.
    60→
    61→### When TDD Doesn't Apply
    62→
    63→Skip TDD for:
    64→
    65→- Pure UI/styling changes (visual verification instead)
    66→- Config file changes
    67→- Documentation updates
    68→- Trivial one-line fixes where the fix is obvious
    69→
    70→For these, just implement directly and run `bun run typecheck`.
    71→
    72→## Code Quality Standards
    73→
    74→### Must follow
    75→
    76→- **No N+1 queries** — Use denormalized columns or batch queries. Use `sessions.last_user_message_at` instead of correlated subqueries.
    77→- **New query patterns need indexes** — Add to `backend/src/lib/schema.ts` and `agent-server/db/schema.ts`.
    78→- **No hardcoded colors** — Use CSS variables/tokens from `src/global.css`.
    79→- **Zustand selector discipline** — Always use individual selectors, never destructure the whole store.
    80→- **Paginate unbounded collections** — Default page size 50-100.
    81→- **Virtualize lists >30 items** — Use `@tanstack/react-virtual`.
    82→
    83→### Edge cases to always consider
    84→
    85→- What if the database is empty?
    86→- What if the worktree directory was deleted?
    87→- What if the git remote is unreachable?
    88→- What if the agent-server process crashes mid-stream?
    89→- What if two agents write to the same session concurrently? (WAL mode handles this, but verify)
    90→
    91→## After Each Step
    92→
    93→Run validation appropriate to what you changed:
    94→
    95→```bash
    96→# TypeScript changes
    97→bun run typecheck
    98→
    99→# Backend changes
   100→bun run test:backend
   101→
   102→# Agent-server changes
   103→bun run test:agent-server:unit
   104→
   105→# Rust changes
   106→cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib
   107→
   108→# Frontend styling
   109→# → Visual verification in browser
   110→```
   111→
   112→Report any failures immediately and fix before proceeding.
   113→
   114→## Memory Management
   115→
   116→After completing work, update your agent memory with:
   117→
   118→- Patterns you discovered in the codebase
   119→- Testing strategies that worked well
   120→- Common pitfalls you encountered
   121→- Architecture decisions you made and why
   122→

1→---
     2→name: explore
     3→description: Deep-dive into a feature area of the codebase. Traces the full stack from frontend to backend to database. Use when onboarding to an area, understanding a feature, or preparing to modify something.
     4→context: fork
     5→agent: Explore
     6→argument-hint: "[feature area or question]"
     7→---
     8→
     9→Explore and explain the following area of the codebase:
    10→
    11→$ARGUMENTS
    12→
    13→## What to investigate
    14→
    15→1. **Find the entry point**: Locate the frontend component, route handler, or Tauri command
    16→2. **Trace the full stack**:
    17→   - Frontend: component → query hook → service → HTTP/IPC call
    18→   - Backend: route → middleware → service → database query
    19→   - Agent-server: RPC handler → agent → SDK → DB write → frontend notification
    20→   - Rust: command → core module → system call
    21→3. **Map the data flow**: How does data move through the system?
    22→4. **Identify the database tables**: Which tables and columns are involved?
    23→5. **Note the state management**: What's in Zustand vs TanStack Query?
    24→6. **Find the tests**: Where are the tests for this area?
    25→
    26→## Architecture reference
    27→
    28→```
    29→Frontend (src/features/{feature}/)
    30→├── api/{feature}.queries.ts    ← TanStack Query hooks
    31→├── api/{feature}.service.ts    ← HTTP/IPC calls
    32→├── ui/                         ← React components
    33→└── store/                      ← Zustand (UI state only)
    34→
    35→Backend (backend/src/)
    36→├── routes/{feature}.ts         ← REST endpoints
    37→├── services/{feature}.ts       ← Business logic
    38→└── middleware/                  ← Request context
    39→
    40→Agent-server (agent-server/)
    41→├── agents/                     ← Agent handlers
    42→├── db/                         ← Direct SQLite writes
    43→└── frontend-client.ts          ← Notifications → Rust → Frontend
    44→
    45→Rust (src-tauri/src/)
    46→├── commands/{feature}.rs       ← Tauri IPC handlers
    47→└── {feature}.rs                ← Core logic
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Output format
    51→
    52→Produce a clear explanation with:
    53→
    54→- A brief overview of what this feature does
    55→- The full data flow (ideally as a sequence)
    56→- Key files and their responsibilities
    57→- Database tables/queries involved
    58→- Known patterns, gotchas, or technical debt
    59→- Where tests live and what they cover
    60→

1→---
     2→name: risk-tier
     3→description: Classify changed files by risk tier and compute required checks. Use when preparing a PR, assessing change impact, or deciding what to test. Triggers on "risk tier", "risk assessment", "what tests do I need", "change impact".
     4→allowed-tools: Bash(git *)
     5→argument-hint: "[branch]"
     6→---
     7→
     8→Analyze the current changes and classify them by risk tier. Output what checks are required before merge.
     9→
    10→## Risk tier definitions
    11→
    12→### Tier 1 — Critical (database, core infra, agent communication)
    13→
    14→Paths:
    15→
    16→- `backend/src/lib/schema.ts` — database schema, all indexes and triggers
    17→- `backend/src/lib/database.ts` — database connection and initialization
    18→- `agent-server/db/schema.ts` — agent-server's schema mirror
    19→- `agent-server/db/index.ts` — agent-server database access
    20→- `src-tauri/src/main.rs` — app initialization and lifecycle
    21→- `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` — module exports
    22→- `src-tauri/src/socket*.rs` — agent-server socket relay
    23→- `src-tauri/src/process*.rs` — process lifecycle management
    24→- `agent-server/index.ts` — agent-server entry point
    25→- `agent-server/rpc-connection.ts` — JSON-RPC communication
    26→- `agent-server/protocol.ts` — shared message types
    27→
    28→Required checks:
    29→
    30→- `bun run typecheck`
    31→- `bun run test` (all backend + agent-server tests)
    32→- `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib`
    33→- `bun run build:agent-server` (verify agent-server builds)
    34→- Manual smoke test of app startup
    35→- Code review by senior developer
    36→
    37→### Tier 2 — High (business logic, data flow, git operations)
    38→
    39→Paths:
    40→
    41→- `backend/src/routes/**` — REST API endpoints
    42→- `backend/src/services/**` — business logic services
    43→- `backend/src/middleware/**` — request middleware
    44→- `agent-server/agents/**` — agent handlers (Claude, Codex)
    45→- `agent-server/frontend-client.ts` — frontend notifications
    46→- `src-tauri/src/git.rs` — git operations (libgit2)
    47→- `src-tauri/src/commands/**` — Tauri IPC command handlers
    48→- `src/platform/**` — platform abstraction layer (Tauri IPC, socket)
    49→- `src/features/*/api/**` — feature query hooks and services
    50→
    51→Required checks:
    52→
    53→- `bun run typecheck`
    54→- `bun run test:backend`
    55→- `bun run test:agent-server`
    56→- `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib`
    57→- Code review
    58→
    59→### Tier 3 — Medium (UI features, state management)
    60→
    61→Paths:
    62→
    63→- `src/features/*/ui/**` — feature UI components
    64→- `src/features/*/store/**` — Zustand stores
    65→- `src/shared/**` — shared components
    66→- `src/global.css` — global styles and design tokens
    67→- `src/app/**` — app shell, routing, providers
    68→
    69→Required checks:
    70→
    71→- `bun run typecheck`
    72→- `bun run format:check`
    73→- Visual verification in browser
    74→
    75→### Tier 4 — Low (docs, config, tests, static assets)
    76→
    77→Paths:
    78→
    79→- `src/components/ui/**` — Shadcn base components (low risk but high usage)
    80→- `*.md` — documentation
    81→- `*.json` — config files (except schema/db)
    82→- `**/*.test.*` — test files
    83→- `**/*.stories.*` — storybook stories
    84→- `.github/**` — CI workflows
    85→- `.claude/**` — Claude Code configuration
    86→
    87→Required checks:
    88→
    89→- `bun run typecheck` (if .ts/.tsx)
    90→- `bun run format:check`
    91→
    92→## Process
    93→
    94→1. **Gather changes**: Run `git diff --name-only $ARGUMENTS` (default: compare against main branch using `git diff --name-only main...HEAD`)
    95→2. **Classify each file**: Map every changed file to its risk tier
    96→3. **Compute highest tier**: The overall PR risk is the highest tier among all changed files
    97→4. **List required checks**: Output the union of all required checks for the highest tier
    98→5. **Flag cross-boundary changes**: Warn if changes span both Rust and Node.js, or both backend and agent-server — these need extra attention
    99→
   100→## Output format
   101→
   102→```
   103→## Risk Assessment
   104→
   105→Overall risk: Tier X — [Critical|High|Medium|Low]
   106→
   107→### Changed files by tier
   108→
   109→**Tier 1 (Critical):**
   110→- path/to/file.ts — reason
   111→
   112→**Tier 2 (High):**
   113→- path/to/file.ts — reason
   114→
   115→**Tier 3 (Medium):**
   116→- path/to/file.ts — reason
   117→
   118→**Tier 4 (Low):**
   119→- path/to/file.ts — reason
   120→
   121→### Required checks before merge
   122→
   123→- [ ] `bun run typecheck`
   124→- [ ] `bun run test`
   125→- [ ] ...
   126→
   127→### Warnings
   128→
   129→- Cross-boundary changes detected: [details]
   130→- Schema changes require both backend and agent-server schema sync
   131→```
   132→
   133→$ARGUMENTS
   134→

1→---
     2→name: risk-tier
     3→description: Classify changed files by risk tier and compute required checks. Use when preparing a PR, assessing change impact, or deciding what to test. Triggers on "risk tier", "risk assessment", "what tests do I need", "change impact".
     4→allowed-tools: Bash(git *)
     5→argument-hint: "[branch]"
     6→---
     7→
     8→Analyze the current changes and classify them by risk tier. Output what checks are required before merge.
     9→
    10→## Risk tier definitions
    11→
    12→### Tier 1 — Critical (database, core infra, agent communication)
    13→
    14→Paths:
    15→
    16→- `backend/src/lib/schema.ts` — database schema, all indexes and triggers
    17→- `backend/src/lib/database.ts` — database connection and initialization
    18→- `agent-server/db/schema.ts` — agent-server's schema mirror
    19→- `agent-server/db/index.ts` — agent-server database access
    20→- `src-tauri/src/main.rs` — app initialization and lifecycle
    21→- `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` — module exports
    22→- `src-tauri/src/socket*.rs` — agent-server socket relay
    23→- `src-tauri/src/process*.rs` — process lifecycle management
    24→- `agent-server/index.ts` — agent-server entry point
    25→- `agent-server/rpc-connection.ts` — JSON-RPC communication
    26→- `agent-server/protocol.ts` — shared message types
    27→
    28→Required checks:
    29→
    30→- `bun run typecheck`
    31→- `bun run test` (all backend + agent-server tests)
    32→- `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib`
    33→- `bun run build:agent-server` (verify agent-server builds)
    34→- Manual smoke test of app startup
    35→- Code review by senior developer
    36→
    37→### Tier 2 — High (business logic, data flow, git operations)
    38→
    39→Paths:
    40→
    41→- `backend/src/routes/**` — REST API endpoints
    42→- `backend/src/services/**` — business logic services
    43→- `backend/src/middleware/**` — request middleware
    44→- `agent-server/agents/**` — agent handlers (Claude, Codex)
    45→- `agent-server/frontend-client.ts` — frontend notifications
    46→- `src-tauri/src/git.rs` — git operations (libgit2)
    47→- `src-tauri/src/commands/**` — Tauri IPC command handlers
    48→- `src/platform/**` — platform abstraction layer (Tauri IPC, socket)
    49→- `src/features/*/api/**` — feature query hooks and services
    50→
    51→Required checks:
    52→
    53→- `bun run typecheck`
    54→- `bun run test:backend`
    55→- `bun run test:agent-server`
    56→- `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib`
    57→- Code review
    58→
    59→### Tier 3 — Medium (UI features, state management)
    60→
    61→Paths:
    62→
    63→- `src/features/*/ui/**` — feature UI components
    64→- `src/features/*/store/**` — Zustand stores
    65→- `src/shared/**` — shared components
    66→- `src/global.css` — global styles and design tokens
    67→- `src/app/**` — app shell, routing, providers
    68→
    69→Required checks:
    70→
    71→- `bun run typecheck`
    72→- `bun run format:check`
    73→- Visual verification in browser
    74→
    75→### Tier 4 — Low (docs, config, tests, static assets)
    76→
    77→Paths:
    78→
    79→- `src/components/ui/**` — Shadcn base components (low risk but high usage)
    80→- `*.md` — documentation
    81→- `*.json` — config files (except schema/db)
    82→- `**/*.test.*` — test files
    83→- `**/*.stories.*` — storybook stories
    84→- `.github/**` — CI workflows
    85→- `.claude/**` — Claude Code configuration
    86→
    87→Required checks:
    88→
    89→- `bun run typecheck` (if .ts/.tsx)
    90→- `bun run format:check`
    91→
    92→## Process
    93→
    94→1. **Gather changes**: Run `git diff --name-only $ARGUMENTS` (default: compare against main branch using `git diff --name-only main...HEAD`)
    95→2. **Classify each file**: Map every changed file to its risk tier
    96→3. **Compute highest tier**: The overall PR risk is the highest tier among all changed files
    97→4. **List required checks**: Output the union of all required checks for the highest tier
    98→5. **Flag cross-boundary changes**: Warn if changes span both Rust and Node.js, or both backend and agent-server — these need extra attention
    99→
   100→## Output format
   101→
   102→```
   103→## Risk Assessment

Verify and fix three issues:

1. `apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line ~228 — Comment says "SQLITE_BUSY errors that can occur when the agent-server is concurrently accessing the DB." This is wrong — agent-server has NO DB access. Fix to say "concurrent backend operations".

2. `apps/backend/src/services/workspace-init.service.ts` line ~270 — Comment says "SQLITE_BUSY can fire when agent-server holds the DB". Same problem. Fix to say "concurrent backend operations hold the DB".

3. `test/e2e/e2e-flow.test.cjs` line ~124 — Uses `response.body.agent-server` which is INVALID JavaScript — the hyphen makes it `response.body.agent - server` (subtraction). This is a runtime bug. Check if the /health endpoint even returns an "agent-server" key (read `apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts`). If not, remove the line entirely. If it does, use bracket notation `response.body["agent-server"]`.

Read each file, verify the issues exist, and fix them.

input.content:
---
name: risk-tier
description: Classify changed files by risk tier and compute required checks. Use when preparing a PR, assessing change impact, or deciding what to test. Triggers on "risk tier", "risk assessment", "what tests do I need", "change impact".
allowed-tools: Bash(git *)
argument-hint: "[branch]"
---

Analyze the current changes and classify them by risk tier. Output what checks are required before merge.

## Risk tier definitions

### Tier 1 — Critical (database, core infra, agent communication)

Paths:

- `shared/schema.ts` — database schema, all indexes and triggers (single source of truth)
- `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts` — database connection and initialization
- `apps/desktop/main/index.ts` — app initialization and lifecycle
- `apps/desktop/main/backend-process.ts` — backend process lifecycle management
- `apps/desktop/main/native-handlers.ts` — Electron IPC command handlers
- `apps/agent-server/index.ts` — agent-server entry point
- `apps/agent-server/rpc-connection.ts` — JSON-RPC communication
- `apps/agent-server/protocol.ts` — shared message types

Required checks:

- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run test` (all backend + agent-server tests)
- `bun run build:agent-server` (verify agent-server builds)
- Manual smoke test of app startup
- Code review by senior developer

### Tier 2 — High (business logic, data flow, git operations)

Paths:

- `apps/backend/src/routes/**` — REST API endpoints
- `apps/backend/src/services/**` — business logic services
- `apps/backend/src/middleware/**` — request middleware
- `apps/agent-server/agents/**` — agent handlers (Claude, Codex)
- `apps/agent-server/event-broadcaster.ts` — canonical event fan-out to backend
- `apps/desktop/main/browser-views.ts` — BrowserView management
- `apps/web/src/platform/**` — platform abstraction layer (Electron IPC, WebSocket)
- `apps/web/src/features/*/api/**` — feature query hooks and services

Required checks:

- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run test:backend`
- `bun run test:agent-server`
- Code review

### Tier 3 — Medium (UI features, state management)

Paths:

- `apps/web/src/features/*/ui/**` — feature UI components
- `apps/web/src/features/*/store/**` — Zustand stores
- `apps/web/src/shared/**` — shared components
- `apps/web/src/global.css` — global styles and design tokens
- `apps/web/src/app/**` — app shell, routing, providers

Required checks:

- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run format:check`
- Visual verification in browser

### Tier 4 — Low (docs, config, tests, static assets)

Paths:

- `apps/web/src/components/ui/**` — Shadcn base components (low risk but high usage)
- `*.md` — documentation
- `*.json` — config files (except schema/db)
- `**/*.test.*` — test files
- `**/*.stories.*` — storybook stories
- `.github/**` — CI workflows
- `.claude/**` — Claude Code configuration

Required checks:

- `bun run typecheck` (if .ts/.tsx)
- `bun run format:check`

## Process

1. **Gather changes**: Run `git diff --name-only $ARGUMENTS` (default: compare against main branch using `git diff --name-only main...HEAD`)
2. **Classify each file**: Map every changed file to its risk tier
3. **Compute highest tier**: The overall PR risk is the highest tier among all changed files
4. **List required checks**: Output the union of all required checks for the highest tier
5. **Flag cross-boundary changes**: Warn if changes span both Electron main and Node.js backend, or both backend and agent-server — these need extra attention

## Output format

```
## Risk Assessment

Overall risk: Tier X — [Critical|High|Medium|Low]

### Changed files by tier

**Tier 1 (Critical):**
- path/to/file.ts — reason

**Tier 2 (High):**
- path/to/file.ts — reason

**Tier 3 (Medium):**
- path/to/file.ts — reason

**Tier 4 (Low):**
- path/to/file.ts — reason

### Required checks before merge

- [ ] `bun run typecheck`
- [ ] `bun run test`
- [ ] ...

### Warnings

- Cross-boundary changes detected: [details]
- Schema changes require backend schema + query invalidation review (shared/schema.ts, query-engine, agent-event-handler)
```

$ARGUMENTS

1→# Code Reviewer Memory
     2→
     3→## Key Architecture Patterns (Confirmed)
     4→
     5→- `react-resizable-panels` uses percentage values for `collapsedSize`/`minSize` — pixel↔percent
     6→  conversion via `panelGroupContainerRef` (excludes agent-server strip from container width math)
     7→- `workspaceLayoutStore` uses `version: 9` migrations (v9 added terminal tab state) — increment version when adding persisted fields
     8→- `cn()` uses `twMerge` internally — arbitrary `animate-[...]` classes conflict-resolve correctly
     9→  (last wins), so conditional breathing override pattern with `cn()` works as intended
    10→- `data-slot="resizable-panel"` is the CSS hook for the flex-grow transition in global.css
    11→
    12→## Animation Patterns
    13→
    14→- `strip-breathe` + `strip-settle` keyframes live in `global.css` (multi-component reuse)
    15→- Single-component animations should use Framer Motion inline, NOT global.css keyframes
    16→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
    17→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` + `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` use
    18→  `motion` (pre-existing violation).
    19→
    20→## Dead Code Pattern (Confirmed)
    21→
    22→- When removing a prop from a call site, also check the destructuring site of the hook.
    23→  TypeScript does not error on unused destructured variables — only ESLint's `no-unused-vars` catches this.
    24→
    25→## CSS Architecture
    26→
    27→- `transition: flex-grow` on `[data-slot="resizable-panel"]` — flex-grow IS animatable per CSS spec
    28→- `window-resizing` class disables ALL layout transitions during native window resize
    29→- `[data-resize-handle-active]` sibling/parent selector disables panel transitions during drag
    30→
    31→## Cross-Component Event Bus Pattern
    32→
    33→- `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("insert-to-chat", { detail }))` is the established
    34→  pattern for browser panel → chat input communication.
    35→
    36→## XML Attribute Serialization Risk Pattern
    37→
    38→- `serializeInspectElement` in `parseInspectTags.ts` embeds user-controlled strings (innerText,
    39→  path, tagName, reactComponent) into XML attributes with NO escaping. A `"` breaks parsing.
    40→  Fix: HTML-escape values before embedding.
    41→
    42→## Distribution / CI Patterns
    43→
    44→- `sed -i ''` is macOS/BSD syntax — fails on ubuntu-latest (GNU sed needs `sed -i "..."`).
    45→- Tauri updater `pubkey` empty string `""` disables signature verification entirely (security regression).
    46→- `minimumSystemVersion` for arm64-only builds should be `"11.0"` (Apple Silicon ships with 11.0).
    47→- The app spawns system `node` binary — hardened runtime notarization may need
    48→  `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation` in Entitlements.plist.
    49→
    50→## Sentry / Observability Patterns (Confirmed)
    51→
    52→- **DSN propagation**: Rust `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_RUST")` bakes frontend DSN at compile time.
    53→  `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_NODE")` is baked into the binary and forwarded as `SENTRY_DSN` env var
    54→  to child processes (backend + agent-server). Frontend uses `import.meta.env.VITE_SENTRY_DSN`.
    55→- **`option_env!` with empty string**: When `SENTRY_DSN_RUST` is unset, `option_env!` returns `None`,
    56→  and `.unwrap_or("")` passes `""` to `sentry::init`. The sentry-rust crate treats empty DSN as
    57→  disabled — no panic, no-op client. This pattern is correct and safe.
    58→- **Conditional init in Node.js**: `if (process.env.SENTRY_DSN) { Sentry.init(...) }` means when
    59→  DSN is absent, Sentry is never initialized. `Sentry.captureException()` and `Sentry.close()` called
    60→  on an uninitialized client are safe no-ops in `@sentry/node@10`. Correct pattern.
    61→- **Frontend `enabled: !import.meta.env.DEV`**: Sentry is initialized but disabled in dev mode.
    62→  `captureException()` calls in `errorReporting.ts` are no-ops in dev mode as a result. Good behavior.
    63→- **`sendDefaultPii: true`** is set on all layers — intentional (desktop app, known users).
    64→- **Sentry guard lifetime (Rust)**: `_sentry_guard` in `main()` lives until process exit. Dropping
    65→  it earlier would flush pending events prematurely. The single-underscore prefix suppresses the
    66→  unused-variable warning without dropping it.
    67→- **`Sentry.close(2000)` before `process.exit`**: The 2-second flush window is appropriate. Both
    68→  backend and agent-server implement this correctly in their `uncaughtException` handlers.
    69→- **Hardcoded Sentry org/project in vite.config.ts**: `org: "deus-40"` and
    70→  `project: "deus-desktop-frontend"` are hardcoded. These are metadata identifiers (not secrets).
    71→  Leaking them poses minimal risk but couples the open-source repo to internal Sentry project names.
    72→- **CI missing Sentry secrets**: `release.yml` does not pass `SENTRY_DSN_RUST`, `SENTRY_DSN_NODE`,
    73→  `VITE_SENTRY_DSN`, or `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. Production builds will have Sentry monitoring disabled
    74→  until these are wired into the workflow.
    75→- **Root ErrorBoundary not connected to Sentry**: The outer `<ConditionalErrorBoundary fallback={DashboardError}>`
    76→  at line 126 of App.tsx has NO `onError` prop. React crashes inside MainLayout that are caught by
    77→  that boundary go unreported to Sentry. Only the inner boundary (line 187) calls `reportError`.
    78→- **`@sentry/node` in production dependencies**: `@sentry/node` and `@sentry/vite-plugin` are in
    79→  `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`). `@sentry/vite-plugin` is build-only and should be in
    80→  `devDependencies`. `@sentry/node` is needed at runtime by backend/agent-server, correct in `dependencies`.
    81→
    82→## Experimental Feature Toggle Pattern (Confirmed)
    83→
    84→- `experimental_*` fields are `boolean | undefined` on `Settings` — `undefined` means ON (backwards-compat)
    85→- `isTabVisible(tab, settings)` is exported from `ContentTabBar.tsx`
    86→- `effectiveContentTab` in `MainContent` = `isTabVisible(raw, settings) ? raw : "changes"` (was "code" before Changes rename)
    87→- BUG (unfixed): `isTabVisible` returns `settings?.[key] === true`, which returns `false` while settings is loading.
    88→  Correct: treat `undefined` as `true` for experimental tabs (`val === undefined || val === true`).
    89→
    90→## workspaceLayoutStore Setter Inconsistency (Confirmed Bug)
    91→
    92→- `setLayout` does a three-way merge: `{ ...defaultLayout, ...existingLayout, ...updates }` — correct.
    93→- All individual setters (`setActiveContentTab`, `setSelectedFilePath`, etc.) do only a two-way merge:
    94→  `{ ...(state.layouts[id] || defaultLayout), field: value }`. When a record exists but lacks a new field
    95→  (e.g. after a version bump), the individual setters will NOT backfill from `defaultLayout`.
    96→  Fix: use the same three-way pattern in all individual setters.
    97→- Store is now at version 10 (bumped during Files-as-top-level-tab refactor). `migrate` wipes all layouts.
    98→
    99→## Simulator / Rust Command Patterns (Confirmed)
   100→
   101→- `sim_has_xcode_project` is a `fn` (sync) Tauri command — safe ONLY because Pass 1 (filesystem
   102→  scan) dominates. Pattern: split fast probe (`has_xcode_project_fast`) from full build.
   103→- macOS-only Tauri commands: gated via `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` in both `commands/mod.rs`
   104→  and `main.rs` invoke_handler lists.
   105→- Three-state probe pattern: `null | true | false`. `null` = loading, suppresses button flicker.
   106→
   107→## Simulator State Machine Patterns (Confirmed)
   108→
   109→- `dispatch()` = validated transition; `setSession()` = recovery bypass for external observation.
   110→- `handleStop()` dispatches STOP first (optimistic), then awaits IPC — avoids UI stuck in "active".
   111→- `STREAM_READY` requires `current.udid === event.udid` — prevents start/stop race.
   112→
   113→## Framer Motion Patterns (Confirmed)
   114→
   115→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
   116→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. Using `motion` bypasses lazy loading and pulls in the full
   117→  bundle. Files `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` and `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` currently use `motion`
   118→  (not `m`) — this is a pre-existing violation, not introduced by new code.
   119→- `useReducedMotion()` is the established pattern for honoring prefers-reduced-motion. Used in
   120→  `RepositoryItem`, `ToolUseBlock`, `ThinkingBlock`, `PastedImageCard`, `PastedTextCard`,
   121→  `CloneRepositoryModal`. New animated components should follow the same pattern.
   122→- `AnimatePresence` exit animation bug: if a component conditionally returns `null` before
   123→  `AnimatePresence` renders, the exit animation never fires. Pattern must be:
   124→  `<AnimatePresence>{condition && <m.div exit={...}>...</m.div>}</AnimatePresence>` — NOT
   125→  `{condition ? <AnimatePresence><m.div>...</m.div></AnimatePresence> : null}`.
   126→- `AnimatePresence` inside `.map()` anti-pattern: placing `<AnimatePresence>` as the return value
   127→  of a `.map()` call (one per item) means the entire boundary is recreated when the item's key
   128→  changes. When the condition flips (e.g., `isEditing` changes from item A to item B), the boundary
   129→  for A is torn down in the same commit as its child — `AnimatePresence` never sees the child
   130→  transition and the exit animation does not fire. Fix: single `<AnimatePresence>` outside the
   131→  `.map()`, with stable keys on each animated child element.
   132→
   133→## Global queryClient Defaults (Confirmed)
   134→
   135→- `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` globally in `queryClient.ts` — critical to prevent typing lag.
   136→  `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` only acceptable for git diff queries (intentional).
   137→
   138→## CORS / External Fetch Patterns
   139→
   140→- Statuspage.io APIs include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` — works from WKWebView.
   141→  Third-party dependency: if headers are removed, fetches silently fail with CORS error.
   142→
   143→## Design Token Completeness
   144→
   145→- `bg-accent-green`, `bg-accent-gold`, `bg-accent-red` ARE valid — defined in `@theme`.
   146→- `bg-warning`, `bg-success`, `text-warning` ARE valid — `--color-warning`/`--color-success` in `@theme`.
   147→
   148→## Analytics (PostHog) Patterns
   149→
   150→- Full details in `analytics-patterns.md`
   151→- Consent model: OPT-OUT (`analytics_enabled !== false` = default ON). Comment in `settings.ts` fixed.
   152→- `_enabled` in `track.ts` defaults to `false` (consent-first). No pre-consent tracking window.
   153→- `app_launched` guarded by `appLaunchTracked` ref — fires once per app lifecycle.
   154→- `onboarding_started` guarded by `onboardingTrackedRef` — StrictMode-safe.
   155→- Analytics toggle in GeneralSection.tsx awaits `saveSetting` and reverts on failure.
   156→- `VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` in `.env.example` is safe to commit — PostHog ingestion keys are write-only.
   157→- `posthog-js/react` is a subpath export of `posthog-js` — no separate dep needed.
   158→
   159→## Content Panel Redesign Pattern (Confirmed)
   160→
   161→- "Code" tab renamed to "Changes". "Files" promoted from sub-tab inside Code to a top-level tab.
   162→- `RightSidePanel` monolith replaced by thin `ContentView` router (`app/layouts/ContentView.tsx`).
   163→- `ChangesView` (`features/workspace/ui/ChangesView.tsx`) is self-contained: fetches its own
   164→  `useFileChanges`, `useUncommittedFiles`, `useLastTurnFiles` data — parent only provides `workspace`.
   165→- `AllFilesDiffViewer`: `hideHeader?: boolean` prop (default false) for embedding in ChangesView.
   166→- Changes layout: AllFilesDiffViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | DiffFilesTree (25%).
   167→- Files layout: FileViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | FileBrowserPanel (25%).
   168→- `PersistentTab` (`app/layouts/PersistentTab.tsx`): thin wrapper for always-mounted tabs using
   169→  `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` CSS-hide pattern.
   170→
   171→## Circular Self-Import Pattern (Recurring Risk)
   172→
   173→- `ChangesView.tsx` imports `useFileChanges` et al. from `"@/features/workspace"` — the barrel that
   174→  re-exports `ChangesView` itself. Bundler resolves it, but it is fragile for HMR.
   175→  Fix: import hooks directly from their source (`../api/workspace.queries`, `../hooks/useWorkspaceLayout`).
   176→- Watch for this pattern whenever a component in `features/X/ui/` is also exported from `features/X/index.ts`
   177→  and then imports from `"@/features/X"`.
   178→
   179→## ContentView Data Ownership Pattern
   180→
   181→- `ContentView` is a routing shell — data fetching belongs in leaf tab components, not in the router.
   182→- The `useFileChanges` call inside `ContentView` (for the Files tab) is a leak of this pattern.
   183→  TanStack Query deduplicates the network call, so it is not a perf issue — but ownership is wrong.
   184→  Fix: move into the Files tab branch or into `FileBrowserPanel` itself.
   185→
   186→## Always-Mount CSS Hide Pattern (Confirmed)
   187→
   188→- Browser, Terminal, and Simulator panels use `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` (not `hidden`)
   189→  when inactive — keeps DOM alive so PTY/WebSocket sessions survive tab switches.
   190→- Pattern: `<div className={cn("h-full w-full", activeTab !== "terminal" && "pointer-events-none invisible absolute")}>`
   191→- `offsetParent === null` check in ResizeObserver guards against `fitAddon.fit()` being called when
   192→  terminal container is CSS-hidden (`invisible` sets `visibility: hidden`, which makes `offsetParent` null for
   193→  absolutely-positioned elements but NOT for `invisible` alone — `invisible absolute` together does make
   194→  `offsetParent` null in most cases, but it is fragile; the `offsetWidth/offsetHeight === 0` check is more reliable).
   195→- `partialize` in the layout store strips terminal tab state (tabs/activeId/nextNum) — PTY processes
   196→  don't survive app restarts; zombie tab metadata would reference dead processes.
   197→- The `updateTabs` helper in TerminalPanel captures `nextTerminalNum` from the _render_ snapshot —
   198→  stale-closure risk exists in the `pendingTask`/`pendingCommand` useEffect callbacks because
   199→  `tabs` and `nextTerminalNum` are excluded from their deps arrays (suppressed with eslint-disable).
   200→
   201→## Multi-Workspace Terminal Persistence Pattern (TerminalPanel.tsx)
   202→
   203→- `visitedWorkspaces: Map<workspaceId, workspacePath>` in TerminalPanel state keeps one
   204→  `WorkspaceTerminals` subtree mounted per visited workspace — PTYs survive workspace switches.
   205→- Non-current workspace terminals use same `pointer-events-none invisible absolute h-full w-full`
   206→  CSS-hide pattern inside a `relative` container.
   207→- `WorkspaceTerminals` sub-component uses per-workspace Zustand selectors for isolation.
   208→- Known leak: `visitedWorkspaces` has no eviction path when a workspace is deleted. PTYs for
   209→  deleted workspaces stay alive until TerminalPanel unmounts. Needs workspace deletion event hook.
   210→- `pendingTask` useEffect risk: if task queued just before workspace switch, the tab opens in the
   211→  NEW workspace because `workspaceId` in deps reflects the current workspace at effect fire time.
   212→- Terminal.tsx `initialCommand` is in the main effect dep array but only consumed once at mount.
   213→  Safe in practice because `id` is globally unique (`crypto.randomUUID()`), so effect runs once per terminal.
   214→  Cleaner to exclude `initialCommand` from deps and snapshot it inside the effect.
   215→
   216→## AgentHandler Interface Patterns (Confirmed)
   217→
   218→- Renamed methods: `handleQuery→query`, `handleCancel→cancel`, `handleReset→reset` (completed in #179)
   219→- `AgentCapabilities` struct added to `AgentHandler` interface — all 4 booleans required
   220→- Optional Claude-specific methods on `AgentHandler`: `auth?`, `initWorkspace?`, `getContextUsage?`,
   221→  `updatePermissionMode?` — guarded by optional-chaining at dispatch in `index.ts`
   222→- `ContextUsageParams` in `agent-handler.ts` has shape `{id, options: {cwd, claudeSessionId}}`.
   223→  The call site in `index.ts` passes `request` (type `Omit<ContextUsageRequest, "type">`) which has an
   224→  extra `agentType` field — TypeScript accepts this as structural subtyping, but it means
   225→  `ContextUsageParams` is effectively a subset alias of `ContextUsageRequest`.
   226→- `capabilities` field is declared but never read at dispatch sites — all dispatch guards use optional
   227→  chaining on the method itself (`agent?.auth`), not `agent.capabilities.auth`. The `capabilities`
   228→  object currently serves documentation purposes only (no runtime enforcement).
   229→- `updatePermissionMode` dispatch in `index.ts` silently no-ops when method is absent (no rejection),
   230→  while `auth` and `initWorkspace` reject with an error. Intentionally asymmetric: permission mode
   231→  is fire-and-forget; auth/workspaceInit are request-response.
   232→- Old method name stale references remain in: `claude-session.ts` JSDoc comments (lines 17, 49) and
   233→  three `console.log` strings in `claude-handler.ts` (lines 100, 119, 179). Not runtime bugs.
   234→- Test describe blocks in `claude-handler.test.ts` still use old names: `handleClaudeQuery` (line 144),
   235→  `handleClaudeCancel` (line 768), `handleClaudeUpdatePermissionMode` (line 798).
   236→
   237→## HTTP vs WS Query Parity Gap (PR "Create Workspace from PR or Branch")
   238→
   239→- `GET /workspaces/by-repo` HTTP fallback and query-engine `runQuery("workspaces")` both build `RepoGroup[]` independently.
   240→  When adding a field to one, must add to both. Confirmed: `git_origin_url` added to query-engine but NOT to HTTP route group object.
   241→- Full details in `http-vs-ws-parity.md`
   242→
   243→## createWorkspace Command Return Value (Pre-Existing Bug, #confirmed)
   244→
   245→- `WorkspaceService.create()` returns `as unknown as Workspace` — but the WS ack only has `{ accepted, commandId }`.
   246→- `workspace.id` on the result is `undefined`. `selectWorkspace(undefined)` no-ops silently.
   247→- Pre-existing since bad96ad4 (protocol unification). Does not cause visible breakage because WS subscription pushes snapshot.
   248→- Full details in `create-workspace-command-return.md`
   249→
   250→## Connection State Machine (features/connection)
   251→
   252→- Zustand store in `connectionStore.ts`: CONNECTED → GRACE_PERIOD (2s) → RECONNECTING (30s total) → DISCONNECTED
   253→- `onDisconnected` guard checks `current !== "connected"` — must allow re-entry from `disconnected`
   254→  to handle Retry button flow (forceReconnect fires notifyConnectionChange(false) before new socket opens)
   255→- `forceReconnect()` in platform/ws immediately calls `notifyConnectionChange(false)` — if guard blocks,
   256→  the banner freezes at DISCONNECTED with no recovery until socket connects
   257→- `emitSendAttemptFailed` in `connectionEvents.ts` is a module-level Set-based event bus (no React deps)
   258→- WS error messages that trigger `emitSendAttemptFailed`: both `"not connected"` AND `"disconnected"`
   259→  must be matched — `"WebSocket disconnected"` (from onclose pending-command rejection) will be missed
   260→  if only checking `"not connected"`
   261→- `platform/ws → connectionStore` is safe (no circular import). `session.queries → connectionEvents` is safe.
   262→- `ConnectionBanner` animates `height` (violates project guidelines) — causes panel jitter on 32→44 change
   263→- `ConnectionOrb` color crossfade: CSS `transition-colors` on a class swap is better than Framer Motion
   264→  `backgroundColor` interpolation (avoids paint-per-frame)
   265→
   266→## Mobile Web Layout Patterns
   267→
   268→- Full details in `mobile-layout-patterns.md`
   269→- `MobileLayout` has `key={selectedWorkspace.id}` at call site in MainContent.tsx — remounts on workspace switch, resetting `activeTab` state
   270→- `MobileTab` type defined once in MobileTabBar.tsx, imported in MobileLayout.tsx
   271→- `AllFilesDiffViewer` in mobile code view uses `hideHeader` prop to suppress close button
   272→- `MobileTabBar` safe-area-inset: uses `pb-[env(...)]` which compresses content; needs `min-h` adjustment
   273→- `MobileTabBar` has ARIA semantics: `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, `aria-labelledby`
   274→
   275→## parseContent Type Contract (Confirmed)
   276→
   277→- `parseContent` from `useSessionWithMessages` has type `(content: string) => (ContentBlock | string)[] | string`
   278→  — NOT `ContentBlock[]`. Returns `string` for plain-text user messages and envelope-format cancelled messages.
   279→- Any utility that accepts `parseContent` as a parameter MUST declare the full return type and guard
   280→  with `Array.isArray` before iterating. Declaring a narrower type (`ContentBlock[]`) compiles but
   281→  causes silent data loss at runtime (string is iterated character-by-character, all `isToolUseBlock`
   282→  guards return false).
   283→- Standard guard pattern: `const result = parseContent(msg.content); if (!Array.isArray(result)) continue;`
   284→
   285→## Reverse-Traverse + Flip Pattern for Message Extraction
   286→
   287→- Walking messages in reverse (newest→oldest) and collecting chunks, then calling `.reverse()` to
   288→  restore chronological order is correct at the message level.
   289→- When also walking blocks within each message in reverse, intra-message block order is ALSO reversed
   290→  before the final flip — resulting in wrong per-message block order after `.reverse()`.
   291→- Correct pattern: walk messages in reverse, but walk BLOCKS within each message FORWARD. Either
   292→  `unshift` message-level results to the output array, or accumulate per-message and prepend.
   293→
   294→## TOOL_COLORS Map Completeness
   295→
   296→- Every tool renderer registered in `registerTools.ts` should have a corresponding entry in `TOOL_COLORS`
   297→  in `toolColors.ts`. Missing entries don't cause crashes but leave other components that derive color
   298→  by tool name with `undefined` (falls through to no class).
   299→- When adding a new renderer, add its color entry to `TOOL_COLORS` in the same commit.
   300→
   301→## Radix DropdownMenuTrigger + asChild + onClick Conflict Pattern
   302→
   303→- When `<DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>` wraps a child with its own `onClick`, Radix's Slot merges
   304→  BOTH handlers. If the child's `onClick` calls `setOpen(false)` (to suppress the dropdown for a
   305→  quick-open action), the dropdown STILL opens first (Radix toggle runs), then closes — visible flicker.
   306→- Fix: either don't use `DropdownMenuTrigger` on the quick-open button (use a sibling trigger for
   307→  the chevron only), or put the `DropdownMenuTrigger` on the chevron/separator button only.
   308→
   309→## Last-Used App Persistence Pattern
   310→
   311→- `useLastOpenInApp` / `getLastOpenInAppId` / `setLastOpenInAppId` live in
   312→  `apps/web/src/shared/hooks/useLastOpenInApp.ts` and are exported from `shared/hooks/index.ts`.
   313→- Pattern: `setLastAppId(appId)` must be called at EVERY `openIn(appId, path)` call site.
   314→  `WorkspaceHeader.tsx`'s `HeaderOpenButton` currently skips this call — Cmd+O will not reflect
   315→  choices made from that dropdown.
   316→
   317→## Spurious useEffect Deps Pattern
   318→
   319→- `useKeyboardShortcuts` includes `selectedWorkspace` and `modalStates` in its `useEffect` deps
   320→  array but neither is read inside the effect body. This causes the global keydown listener to
   321→  be torn down and re-registered on every workspace switch and modal state change.
   322→  Fix: remove those deps, or move logic that needs them into the callback params.
   323→
   324→## getInstalledAppsList() Race / Cache Pattern (native-handlers.ts)
   325→
   326→- Pre-fix risk: `cachedInstalledApps` alone allowed simultaneous first-load callers to duplicate the
   327→  full `mdfind` loop and race on shared temp icon paths.
   328→- Current pattern: pair `cachedInstalledApps` with `inflightPromise` so concurrent callers share the
   329→  same discovery pass until the cache is populated. `.finally()` clears `inflightPromise` on both
   330→  success and failure paths.
   331→
   332→## mdfind bundleId Query Safety
   333→
   334→- `mdfind` in `native-handlers.ts` interpolates `app.bundleId` directly into the query string:
   335→  `kMDItemCFBundleIdentifier == '${app.bundleId}'`. Values come from SUPPORTED_APPS which is
   336→  hardcoded, so this is safe. No user-controlled data reaches mdfind.
   337→
   338→## See Also
   339→
   340→- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, agent-server resume
   341→- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns
   342→

input.old_string:
## Distribution / CI Patterns

- `sed -i ''` is macOS/BSD syntax — fails on ubuntu-latest (GNU sed needs `sed -i "..."`).
- Tauri updater `pubkey` empty string `""` disables signature verification entirely (security regression).
- `minimumSystemVersion` for arm64-only builds should be `"11.0"` (Apple Silicon ships with 11.0).
- The app spawns system `node` binary — hardened runtime notarization may need
  `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation` in Entitlements.plist.
input.new_string:
## Monorepo Source Path Map (CRITICAL — always verify before writing path globs)

- `shared/schema.ts` — DB schema, indexes, triggers (single source of truth, NOT in backend/lib/)
- `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts` — DB connection + initialization
- `apps/backend/src/routes/**`, `apps/backend/src/services/**`, `apps/backend/src/middleware/**`
- `apps/agent-server/index.ts`, `apps/agent-server/rpc-connection.ts`, `apps/agent-server/protocol.ts`
- `apps/agent-server/agents/**`, `apps/agent-server/event-broadcaster.ts`
- `apps/desktop/main/index.ts` — Electron app init + lifecycle
- `apps/desktop/main/backend-process.ts` — backend process management
- `apps/desktop/main/native-handlers.ts` — Electron IPC handlers (replaces Tauri commands)
- `apps/desktop/main/browser-views.ts` — BrowserView management
- `apps/web/src/platform/**` — platform abstraction (IPC, WebSocket)
- `apps/web/src/features/*/api/**`, `apps/web/src/features/*/ui/**`, `apps/web/src/features/*/store/**`
- `apps/web/src/shared/**`, `apps/web/src/global.css`, `apps/web/src/app/**`
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/**` — Shadcn base components
- `src-tauri/` DOES NOT EXIST — the project migrated from Tauri (Rust) to Electron. No Cargo.toml, no .rs files.
- `agent-server/db/` DOES NOT EXIST — agent-server is stateless (no DB). Schema lives in `shared/schema.ts`.

## Distribution / CI Patterns

- `sed -i ''` is macOS/BSD syntax — fails on ubuntu-latest (GNU sed needs `sed -i "..."`).
- The app is Electron (not Tauri). No Cargo.toml, no `cargo test`, no entitlements.plist at `src-tauri/`.
- The app spawns system `node` binary for backend/agent-server child processes.
